clauses.description: [OPTION A (arbitration):    33.  DISPUTE RESOLUTION  (a) Any dispute arising out of or in connection with the Contract Document, including any question regarding its existence, validity or termination and any dispute relating to any non contractual obligations arising out of or in connection with it, shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration administered by the Singapore International Arbitration Centre (SIAC) in accordance with the Arbitration Rules of SIAC (SIAC Rules), which Rules are deemed to be incorporated by reference into this clause.   (b) The number of arbitrators shall be [one].   (c) The seat, or legal place, of arbitration shall be Singapore.   (d) The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English.   (e) This arbitration agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accordance with Singapore law.]    [OPTION B (court jurisdiction):     The Parties submit and agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Singapore courts.]    For New York, use the below dispute resolution clause:  (b)  Each Party submits to the non exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of New York and the United States District Court located in the Borough of Manhattan in New York city, and waives any objection which it may have at any time to the laying of venue of any proceedings brought in any such court, waives any claim that such proceedings have been brought in an inconvenient forum and further waives the right to object, with respect to such proceedings, that such court does not have any jurisdiction over such party; and agrees, to the extent permitted by applicable law, that the bringing of proceedings in anyone or more jurisdictions will not preclude the bringing of proceedings in any other jurisdiction.  Waiver of Jury Trial: Each Party hereby irrevocably waives any and all rights to trial by jury in any proceedings.

clauses.description: 13. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY  13.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this clause 13, each Party (and its licensors) shall retain ownership of its Background IP, and the Parties acknowledge and agree that nothing in the Contract Document is intended to transfer to any Party any Background IP of another Party.  13.2 The Supplier's provision (and the Customer's use and receipt) of the Supplies shall be subject to the specific licence terms set out in the relevant Supply Schedule or Order Form.  13.3 The Supplier hereby grants to the Customer and each Customer Group member a worldwide, non exclusive, perpetual, royalty free, irrevocable licence to use, modify, adapt and reproduce the Supplier's Background IP and to create derivative works of the Supplier's Background IP to the extent necessary to receive the benefit of the Supplies (but not for commercialisation purposes).  13.4 The Customer and each Customer Group member may:  13.4.1 sublicense any or all of the rights referred to in clause 13.3 to Third Parties solely for the purposes of, and only to the extent necessary to allow, those Third Parties to provide services to the Customer or any Customer Group member (but not for commercialisation purposes); or  13.4.2 sublicense or assign any and all of the rights referred to in clause 13.3 to any Customer Group member, to the extent necessary to enable them to receive the benefit of the Supplies (but not for commercialisation purposes).  13.5 Subject to clause 13.6, if, in the course of providing the Supplies to the Customer, the Supplier provides or makes available to the Customer any Materials in which the Intellectual Property Rights of a Third Party subsist ('Third Party IP'), the Supplier shall procure for the Customer and each Customer Group member a licence to such Third Party IP on the same terms that the Supplier licenses the Supplier's Background IP to the Customer and each Customer Group member.  13.6 If the Supplier is unable to license the Third Party IP on the same terms that the Supplier licenses the Supplier's Background IP, the Supplier shall provide the Customer with written details of the licence terms under which the Supplier proposes to license the Third Party IP. If the licence terms are acceptable to the Customer, they will form part of the Order Form. If the licence terms are not acceptable to the Customer:  13.6.1 the Supplier shall use its reasonable endeavours to renegotiate the licence terms so that they are acceptable to the Customer, in which case the renegotiated licence terms which are acceptable to the Customer will form part of the Order Form; and  13.6.2 if the Supplier is unable to renegotiate the licence terms so that they are acceptable to the Customer, the Supplier shall propose an alternative solution that does not require the Third Party IP.  13.7 The Supplier shall not introduce any Third Party IP into the Customer Group Systems without the prior written consent of the Customer.  13.8 The Customer hereby grants to the Supplier a non exclusive, royalty free, revocable, non transferable licence to use the Customer's Background IP to the extent required to perform the Supplier's obligations under the relevant Contract Document.  13.9 The Supplier represents, warrants and undertakes that:  13.9.1 the Supplier is able to license the Supplier's Background IP as described in clause 13.3;  13.9.2 the Supplier has and will continue to have all rights in and to its tools necessary to perform its obligations under the Contract Document;  13.9.3 the provision and use (and, where applicable, assignment) of the Supplies, the Supplier's Background IP, Deliverables and Third Party IP in accordance with and in the manner intended or permitted by the relevant Contract Document will not infringe the Intellectual Property Rights of any Third Party or breach any Applicable Law; and  13.9.4 neither the Customer nor any Customer Group member, nor any other person acting at the direction or with the permission of the Customer, will be liable to pay any Third Party any licence or other fee in respect of the provision and use (or, where applicable, assignment) of the Supplies, Third Party IP, Supplier's Background IP or Deliverables in accordance with and in the manner intended or permitted by the relevant Contract Document.  13.10 The Customer represents and warrants that:  13.10.1 the Customer is able to license the Customer's Background IP as described in clause 13.8; and  13.10.2 the use of the Customer's Background IP in accordance with and in the manner intended or permitted by the relevant Contract Document will not infringe the Intellectual Property Rights of any Third Party or breach any Applicable Law.  13.11 The Supplier shall notify the Customer and each relevant member of the Customer Group as soon as reasonably practicable upon becoming aware of any infringement or suspected or alleged infringement by the Supplies (or any Deliverables) of the Intellectual Property Rights of any Third Party.

clauses.description: 30. SUBCONTRACTING  30.1 The Supplier shall not subcontract any of its obligations under the Contract Document without the Customer's prior written consent.  30.2 Prior to subcontracting any of its obligations under the Contract Document, the Supplier shall notify the Customer of its intention to subcontract its obligations and shall provide the Customer with details of any potential subcontractors, including any change to Subcontractors.  30.3 Where the Supplier engages a Subcontractor under the Contract Document, the Supplier shall:  30.3.1 provide the Customer with such details on the Subcontractors reasonably required by the Customer from time to time, including the screening conducted by the Supplier in respect of such Subcontractors, the location of the Subcontractors and the controls put in place by the Supplier to ensure that such Subcontractors perform their relevant tasks in accordance with the Contract Document;  30.3.2 ensure that Subcontractors comply with all of the applicable terms and conditions of the Contract Document applying to the Supplier and references to Supplier in the Contract Document shall instead be to that Subcontractor;  30.3.3 remain liable to the Customer for the performance of the subcontracted obligations performed by Subcontractors under the Contract Document as if acts or omissions of a Subcontractor were acts or omissions of the Supplier;  30.3.4 ensure that all subcontracts with Subcontractors are made in writing and are consistent with the terms and conditions of the Contract Document;  30.3.5 ensure that subcontracts with Subcontractors are capable of being novated, transferred or assigned to the Customer without any additional costs to the Customer; and  30.3.6 promptly notify the Customer in the event of any breach by the Subcontractor of the terms and conditions of the relevant subcontract.  30.4 The Supplier shall notify the Customer of any changes to Subcontractors in accordance with clause 30.2 and shall seek the Customer's consent to such changes in Subcontractors prior to such change. The Customer shall have the right to object to such changes in Subcontractors.  30.5 If the Supplier engages a Subcontractor or makes any changes to Subcontractors without having obtained the Customer's consent in accordance with this clause 30, this shall constitute a material breach of the Contract Document and, without prejudice to any other remedy the Customer may have under the Contract Document or at law, the Customer shall be entitled to terminate the Contract Document in accordance with clause 20.3.1.  30.6 If:  30.6.1 a Subcontractor commits a material breach of any relevant subcontract;  30.6.2 the Customer reasonably believes that a Subcontractor's performance of its obligations is unsatisfactory (and has not been rectified within a reasonable time period);  30.6.3 the Customer reasonably believes that the continuing engagement of a Subcontractor is materially damaging, or is likely to materially damage, the reputation or brand of any Customer Group member; or  30.6.4 the Supplier engages a Subcontractor in breach of this clause 30,  then the Customer may, by notice, require the Supplier to promptly terminate the subcontract with the Subcontractor in question, and require the Supplier to have those Supplies performed by an alternative Subcontractor or by the Supplier itself, as soon as reasonably practicable and the Supplier shall comply with any such requirement from the Customer.

clauses.description: 29.  ASSIGNMENT   29.1  Subject to clauses 29.2 and 29.3, neither Party may assign, transfer, novate or otherwise deal with its rights or obligations under the Contract Document without the other Party's prior written consent (which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld).  29.2  The Customer may assign, transfer, novate or otherwise deal with the Customer's rights or obligations under the Contract Document in whole or in part at any time to any Customer Group member or any successor of all or part of the Customer's business without the Supplier's consent.   29.3  If required by the Customer, the Supplier shall enter into documentation in a form provided by the Customer to give effect to the assignment, transfer or novation of, or other dealing with, the Contract Document in accordance with clause 29.2.

clauses.description: 35.5 Third party rights  35.5.1 The Contract Document does not create or confer any rights or benefits enforceable by any person not a party to it except that to the extent any rights or benefits are conferred to members of the Customer Group:  (A) each member of the Customer Group shall have the right to enforce any rights or benefits conferred on it in the Contract Document against the Supplier directly; and  (B) each member of the Customer Group shall have the right to enforce the rights or benefits of any indemnity, limitation or exclusion of liability in the Contract Document against the Supplier directly.  35.5.2 No consent from the persons referred to in this clause is required for the Parties to vary or rescind the Contract Document (whether or not in a way that varies or extinguishes rights or benefits in favour of such Third Parties).

clauses.description: 3.  LICENCE OF SOFTWARE AND DOCUMENTATION  3.1  Subject to any restrictions set out in the Order Form, the Supplier hereby grants to:   3.1.1  each Customer Group member;  3.1.2  [each customer of a Customer Group member to the extent necessary to receive the full benefit of the Software and Documentation;] and  3.1.3  any Customer Service Provider (provided that such Customer Service Provider's use shall be limited solely to providing such goods or services to any Customer Group member),   (each, a 'Licensee'), a fully paid up, worldwide, irrevocable, non exclusive, licence (with the right to grant sub licences) for the Licence Term to Use the Software in object code form and to Use the Documentation.  3.2  The licence granted under clause 3.1 shall include the rights to:  3.2.1  access and Use the Software and Documentation, including in operation with other software, hardware, systems, networks and services;  3.2.2  generate, print, copy, upload, download, store and otherwise process all graphical user interface, audio, visual, digital, and other output, displays and content as may result from any use of the Software and Documentation; and  3.2.3  access and Use the Software and Documentation for non production uses and applications to the extent necessary or useful for the effective use of the Software and Documentation.  3.3  The Customer and each Customer Group member may permit Customer Service Providers and customers to Use the Software and Documentation within the scope of the permissions granted under this clause 3.  3.4  Unless expressly set out in the Contract Document, the scope of the licence granted under clause 3.1 is not restricted by:  3.4.1  the number of Licensees;  3.4.2  the number of users;  3.4.3  the number or volume of transactions;  3.4.4  the number of interfaces;  3.4.5  the number of employees or the revenue of any Licensee;   3.4.6  the number or location of suppliers of the Licensee;  3.4.7  the equipment on which the Software may be used or from which it may be accessed from time to time, including, without limitation, the number, size or specification of CPUs; or    3.4.8  the location of equipment on which the Software may be used or from which it may be accessed.  3.5  Unless expressly set out in the Contract Document, the Charges payable under this Software Purchase Schedule shall not be calculated by reference to any of the items listed in clause 3.4.  3.6  Without limiting any other provision of the Contract Document, in the event the Supplier:  3.6.1  removes any features or functionality from the Software and subsequently offers those features or functionality in a new or different product (whether directly or indirectly or through a third party);   3.6.2  stops licensing the licensed Software other than as bundled or otherwise combined with other software;   3.6.3  elects to unbundle or otherwise separately licence any Software that is a suite or other combination of software products; or  3.6.4  discontinues the general availability of support services for the Software and subsequently offers a new or different product as a replacement and/or successor for that Software (whether directly or indirectly or through a third party),  then the licence granted under clause 3.1 shall be deemed to include the relevant portion of or the entirety of (as necessary to ensure the continuance of all functionality originally licensed to the Licensees), any new, different, bundled or unbundled products, as applicable, without payment of any additional licence fee or charge.  3.7  Notwithstanding clause 3.6, the Customer shall in any event be entitled to exchange the affected Software for any relevant replacement and/or successor software product, in the context of clause 3.6.4, without payment of any additional licence fee or charge.  3.8  Any licence granted under clause 3.1 that is granted on a perpetual basis in accordance with the Order Form shall survive the termination or expiry of the Contract Document.

clauses.description: 3.  ACCESS AND USE  3.1  The Supplier hereby grants to the Customer and each member of the Customer Group a non exclusive, transferrable, irrevocable, sub licensable, royalty free, worldwide, right and license to access and use, and to permit the Authorised Users to access and use the Cloud Services and Documentation, including anything developed by the Supplier and delivered to the Customer as a part of the Cloud Services, during the Service Term for the Permitted Purposes. This shall include the right and license to:  3.1.1  access and use the Cloud Services, including in operation with other software, hardware, systems, networks and services;  3.1.2  process all graphical user interface, audio, visual, digital, and other output, displays and content as may result from any access to or use of the Cloud Services; and  3.1.3  access and use the Cloud Services for non production uses and applications to the extent necessary or useful for the effective use of the Cloud Services.  3.2  The Customer may:  3.2.1  sublicense any or all of the rights referred to in clause 3.1 to Third Parties solely for the purposes of, and only to the extent necessary to allow such Third Parties to provide services to the Customer or any Customer Group member (but not for commercialisation purposes); or  3.2.2  sublicense or assign any and all of the rights referred to in clause 3.1 to any Customer Group member, to the extent necessary to enable them to receive the benefit of the Cloud Services (but not for commercialisation purposes).

clauses.description: 22. LIABILITY  22.1 Nothing in the Contract Document shall exclude or limit the liability of either Party in respect of:  22.1.1 death or personal injury caused by its negligence (including negligence by its employees, agents or contractors);  22.1.2 Third Party property damage;  22.1.3 any indemnity given in the Contract Document;  22.1.4 a deliberate personal repudiatory breach;  22.1.5 dishonesty, or the tort of deceit or wilful misconduct or neglect by its employees, agents or contractors;  22.1.6 fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; or  22.1.7 any liability which may not otherwise be limited or excluded under Applicable Law,  and the Parties acknowledge that any Charges deductions (including liquidated damages and Service Credits (if applicable)) are excluded from all calculations of liability caps under the Contract Document.  22.2 Subject to clauses 22.1, 22.3 and [22.5], in no event will either Party be liable to the other for indirect or consequential damages arising out of or in connection with the Contract Document.  22.3 Subject to clauses 22.1 and 22.2, the liability of each Party to the other arising out of or in connection with the Contract Document whether arising from contract, tort, negligence or otherwise shall be limited as follows:  22.3.1 for loss of or damage to physical property, the limit for any one event shall be [insert currency and amount] or, if ascertainable and if greater, three hundred percent (300%) of the total amount of the Charges which would have been payable (had the Order Form been fully and properly performed) to the Supplier by the Customer under the Order Form; and  22.3.2 for any other liability, the aggregate liability for any one event shall be limited to [insert currency and amount] or, if ascertainable and if greater, three hundred percent (300%) of the total amount of the Charges which would have been payable (had the Order Form been fully and properly performed) to the Supplier by the Customer under the Order Form.  22.3 Subject to clauses 22.1 and 22.2, the liability of each Party to the other arising out of or in connection with the Contract Document whether arising from contract, tort, negligence or otherwise shall be limited as follows:  22.3.1 for loss of or damage to physical property, the limit for any one event shall be [insert currency and amount] or, if ascertainable and if greater, three hundred percent (300%) of the total amount of the Charges which would have been payable (had the Contract Document been fully and properly performed) to the Supplier by the Customer under the Contract Document; and  22.3.2 for any other liability, the aggregate liability for any one event shall be limited to [insert currency and amount] or, if ascertainable and if greater, three hundred percent (300%) of the total amount of the Charges which would have been payable (had the Contract Document been fully and properly performed) to the Supplier by the Customer under the Contract Document.  22.4 A reference to the Charges in clause 22.3 means the Charges without taking into account any liquidated damages or Service Credits (if any) or under any provision in the Contract Document where a debt or other payment is due, and includes all Charges that would become payable if the Customer and the Supplier were to continue to properly perform all of their respective obligations under the Contract Document until the end of the [Order Form Term / GTCs Term], and is not limited to amounts which have become due and payable.  22.5 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, the following is a non exhaustive list of the types of Losses that are recoverable by the Customer as damages from the Supplier under the Contract Document:  22.5.1 the reasonable cost of purchasing, installing, testing and implementing alternative software and/or services used in substitution for the Supplies;  22.5.2 the reasonable cost of restoring lost or corrupted data or Customer Confidential Information, including recreating data in circumstances where the data is no longer reasonably available in the form in which it existed at the time of the loss or corruption, provided that the data has been backed up by or on behalf of the Customer in accordance with a reasonable back up policy (but that proviso does not apply if the Supplier is responsible for backing up that data under the Contract Document);  22.5.3 the reasonable cost of implementing and performing workarounds for any non performing Supplies or replacing lost, stolen or damaged Supplies;  22.5.4 the reasonable cost of procuring replacement products or services, or products or services designed to rectify defects in the Supplies from an alternate source, to the extent that these costs exceed the Charges for the Supplies;  22.5.5 additional wages, overtime and expenses (including travel, lodging and wages) over and above the amounts paid to the Supplier directly and reasonably incurred by the Customer Group in performing or rectifying the Supplies that the Supplier has failed to perform and managing a third party's or the Supplier's performance or rectification of the Supplies that the Supplier has failed to perform;  22.5.6 payments or penalties imposed by a Regulatory Authority or any additional compliance costs incurred as a result of a failure by the Supplier to perform its obligations under the Contract Document;  22.5.7 any other claim made against the Customer by a Third Party arising as a result of an act or omission by the Supplier in connection with the Contract Document;  22.5.8 all costs incurred to remedy a breach of obligations (including fundamental obligations of the Supplier) or to remedy any act, default, omission, negligence or statement of the Supplier or the Supplier Personnel in connection with the subject matter of the Contract Document;  22.5.9 in respect of any negligent act or omission of the Supplier or any of the Supplier Personnel arising out of or in connection with the Services or the Contract Document, the Customer's right to recover Loss under the Contract Document includes the right to recover for any Losses flowing from such negligent act or omission, including Consequential Loss; and  22.5.10 any Losses arising from a breach of clauses 15 (Confidential Information), 16 (Publicity), 17 (Conflict of Interest) or the Regulatory Schedule by the Supplier or Supplier Personnel.  22.6 The Customer may recover directly from the Supplier any damages or losses suffered or costs incurred by other members of the Customer Group as a result of any failure of the Supplier to comply with the terms of the Contract Document or at Customer's election require that such damages be paid direct to the relevant member of the Customer Group.

clauses.description: 4. SUPPLIER CHARTER  4.1 It is a condition of the Supplier's engagement that it reads, understands and complies with, and procures its Personnel and professional advisers involved in the delivery of the Supplies to read and comply with the Charter. The Charter is a formal position statement that details the behaviours that the Customer expects from its suppliers, their personnel and their professional advisers involved in the provision of goods and services to the Customer Group in relation to: Ethics, Anti Bribery and Corruption, Human Rights, Environment, Health and Safety Standards, Labour and Protecting the Environment.  4.2 The Supplier acknowledges and agrees that the Customer may investigate any behaviour that the Supplier, Supplier Personnel or professional advisers of the Supplier exhibit that is misaligned with the Charter. If the Supplier, the Supplier Personnel, or professional advisers is in breach of the Charter, the Customer may, without prejudice to any other remedy available to the Customer, terminate the Contract Document immediately by notice to the Supplier.

clauses.clause_key: Supplier Deployed Personnel / Non Employed Workers
clauses.description: 10. SUPPLIER PERSONNEL  10.1 To the extent Supplier Personnel are involved in providing the Supplies to the Customer, the following provisions shall apply in respect of such Supplier Personnel and shall be in addition to the terms and conditions with respect to Supplier Personnel set out in the applicable Supply Schedules. The Supplier shall:  10.1.1 procure that the Supplier Personnel comply with the Contract Document and, upon request by the Customer, ensure that all Supplier Personnel execute such agreements or undertakings as required for compliance with the Contract Document;  10.1.2 be fully responsible and liable for all acts and omissions of any Supplier Personnel;  10.1.3 ensure that Supplier Personnel performing the Contract Document are qualified, efficient, trained, competent and fully supervised at all times and possess suitable skills, expertise, knowledge and experience for the delivery of the Supplies;  10.1.4 where applicable, ensure that all Supplier Personnel have all applicable regulatory clearances and approvals required to enable the performance of the tasks assigned to them;  10.1.5 ensure that all Supplier Personnel are appropriately screened in accordance with the Supplier's policies and procedures and otherwise in accordance with Good Industry Practice;    10.1.6 ensure that adequate numbers of Supplier Personnel are allocated to the performance of the Supplier's obligations at all times; and  10.1.7 ensure that any Supplier Personnel who has become incapable of performing their duties in accordance with the Contract Document is replaced in a timely manner.  10.2 The Supplier shall immediately notify the Customer on becoming aware of any Supplier Personnel:  10.2.1 contravening any Applicable Law or the requirements of the Contract Document;  10.2.2 acting or failing to act in any way that endangers the health or safety of any person; or  10.2.3 engaging in serious misconduct,  in each case in relation to the provision of the Supplies, and immediately replace the relevant Supplier Personnel with a person of comparable skill, experience, expertise and qualification, as approved by the Customer.  10.3 The Supplier shall, at the reasonable request of the Customer, replace any Supplier Personnel involved in the provision of the Supplies with another Supplier Personnel acceptable to the Customer.  10.4 The removal or replacement of any member of the Supplier Personnel for any reason shall not affect or relieve the Supplier from any of its obligations under the Contract Document.  10.5 The Customer shall under no circumstances be liable to the Supplier or to any Supplier Personnel in respect of any Loss occasioned by the removal or replacement of any Supplier Personnel pursuant to this clause 10 or otherwise in accordance with the Contract Document.  10.6 The Supplier shall discharge and perform all obligations and liabilities that may be imposed on it by Applicable Law or otherwise by reason of the Supplier Personnel being employed or engaged by the Supplier. Without prejudice to the generality of this clause 10, the Supplier shall duly comply with all legal and customary requirements under Applicable Law to withhold and pay taxes and social security premiums (or any similar amounts) with respect to salary, remuneration, benefits and other costs payable to and in connection with the Supplier Personnel.  10.7 The Supplier shall at all times, whether during or after the Contract Document Term, defend, indemnify, hold harmless and keep indemnified the Indemnified Parties from and against Losses brought against or incurred by any of the Indemnified Parties (including any Losses which any of the Indemnified Parties agrees to pay by way of settlement or compromise) to the extent related to or arising, directly or indirectly, out of:  10.7.1 any claim, demand or action made or brought by any Supplier Personnel;  10.7.2 labour entitlements, taxes and social security premiums or other similar amounts (including the employer's part thereof) which any of the Indemnified Parties may at any time be required to pay or be obliged to withhold in respect of Supplier Personnel in connection with or as a consequence of the Contract Document;  10.7.3 any and all costs, penalties and interests which may be levied against any of the Indemnified Parties with regard to any such labour entitlements, taxes and social security premiums; and  10.7.4 any Losses which any of the Indemnified Parties suffers or incurs (including any Losses which an Indemnified Party agrees to pay by way of settlement or compromise or otherwise) to the extent related to or arising, directly or indirectly, out of any employment related claim by any Supplier Personnel, including for alleged infringement of anti discrimination laws.      15.4.1 to the Recipient Party's Personnel and professional advisers to the extent necessary to enable the Recipient Party to exercise its rights and perform its obligations under the Contract Document, provided that prior to disclosure such Personnel and professional advisers have entered into an appropriate written confidentiality undertaking or non disclosure agreement with the Recipient Party to treat and keep such Confidential Information confidential on terms no less onerous than contained in the Contract Document;    11. SCREENING  The Supplier shall comply with the requirements set out in the Screening Procedures and Reporting Annexure attached to this Schedule.    SCREENING PROCEDURES AND REPORTING ANNEXURE    1  Definitions  Unless the context otherwise requires, capitalised terms used but not defined in this Annexure shall have the meaning given to them in the Contract Document. For the purposes of this Annexure, the following capitalised words shall have the following meanings:    Adverse Findings has the meaning defined in paragraph 2.5 of this Annexure.    Confidentiality Undertaking means the Customer's standard written letter or document setting out the Supplier Deployed Personnel's undertaking of confidentiality to the Customer as required by the Customer to be signed by the Supplier Deployed Personnel for the purposes of compliance with Applicable Laws in the country where the services are provided.    Customer Group Non Employed Workers Privacy Statement means the current Customer Group statement or notice as may be updated by the Customer from time to time, which sets out how the Customer will use and process the Supplier Deployed Personnel's Personal Data.    Customer Group Screening Check Standards means the current mandatory minimum verification standard or enhanced background screening checks relevant for the role or service to be performed and the Self Certification Declaration required under the Customer Group Staff Screening Policy and Procedure as may updated from time to time to ensure compliance with the Policy Standards.    Customer Group Systems means any and all information technology and communications systems and networks used by the Customer Group, including but not limited to software, databases, firewalls, servers, hardware, middleware and CPUs.    Customer Service Provider means any service provider, contractor or other third party who is engaged by or who contracts with a Customer Group member to provide, operate or otherwise manage services for the benefit of any Customer Group member and/or third party customers of the Customer Group.    Data Protection Laws means all Applicable Laws and all statutes, laws, secondary legislation, regulations, guidelines and industry standards applicable to the Customer Group and/or the Supplier Group pertaining to privacy (including EU GDPR and UK GDPR), confidentiality, banking secrecy and/or the protection of Personal Data or Customer Data, all as from time to time amended, extended, re enacted or consolidated.     Policy Standards means the purpose and scope of the Customer Group's Staff Screening Policy to protect the Customer Group from a range of people risks, including:  (a) potential for internal crime and dishonesty of individuals attempting to avoid detection of an adverse financial or criminal record  (b) potential for breach of external rules and regulations or damage to health, security and safety of staff, customers or third parties arising from internal failures or effects of external events such as risk of infiltration by terrorist or criminal organisations; and in relation to staff to:  •  confirm integrity, honesty and reliability  •  comply with financial and non financial regulatory requirements  •  identify attempts to conceal relevant information or otherwise misrepresent themselves  •  identify risk areas for increased potential internal crime and dishonesty.    Screening Checks has the meaning given to it in paragraph 2.1 of this Annexure.    Screening Verification has the meaning given to it in paragraph 2.11 of this Annexure.    Self Certification Declaration means the responses to the current self declaration questions set out in the Self Certification Letter provided by the Customer to the Supplier relevant to the country where the services are to be performed by the Supplier Deployed Personnel to ensure compliance with the Policy Standards.    Supplier Deployed Personnel  has the meaning given to it in paragraph 2.1 of this Annexure.    Terms of Use means the terms of use and/or the Customer Group third party licensor end user licence agreement relating to the access and use of the Vendor Management System.    Vendor Management System or VMS  means the Customer Group System(s) used by the Customer for management of Supplier Deployed Personnel within the Customer Group.     2  Customer Group Screening Standards  2.1  The Supplier shall ensure that background screening checks have been carried out on the personnel it is providing under the Contract Document in accordance with the Customer Group Screening Check Standards ('Screening Checks'), where such personnel are assigned or deployed to provide service(s) to any Customer Group member under the Contract Document and during the provision of such service:  (a)  will have unsupervised/unescorted access to the Customer Group's premises; and/or  (b)  will have access (whether remotely or otherwise) to the Customer Group Systems,  ('Supplier Deployed Personnel').  2.2  If the existing pre employment background screening checks carried out on the Supplier Deployed Personnel by the Supplier do not meet the Customer Group Screening Check Standards, the Supplier shall conduct such necessary additional Screening Checks in accordance with Applicable Laws prior to commencement of work by the Supplier Deployed Personnel at the Supplier's own cost, unless otherwise agreed by the Customer in writing.  2.3  The Customer Group Screening Check Standards are periodically reviewed by the Customer to ensure they remain up to date to meet applicable legal and regulatory requirements. The Customer shall inform the Supplier in writing of any changes to the Customer Group Screening Check Standards during the term of the Contract Document.  2.4  The Supplier acknowledges and agrees that it may be required by the Customer to re perform Screening Checks (including obtaining new Self Certification Declarations) for the Supplier Deployed Personnel to ensure compliance with the current Customer Group Screening Check Standards.  2.5  It is a condition of the Contract Document that the Supplier provides suitable personnel for the purposes of providing services to the Customer. The Supplier acknowledges and agrees that it will not supply Supplier Deployed Personnel to provide services under the Contract Document who have not been subject to Screening Checks and who are the subject of adverse or unsatisfactory findings under the Screening Checks which demonstrate non compliance with the Policy Standards ('Adverse Findings').  2.6  If any Supplier Deployed Personnel are the subject of Adverse Findings or are unable to complete all of the Screening Checks required, the Supplier shall promptly notify the Customer. In the event that the Customer does not agree that such Adverse Finding is justified or acceptable, the Supplier shall propose an alternative individual with equivalent experience, seniority and qualifications (giving details of the proposed individual's experience, seniority and qualifications) to provide the services.  2.7  The Supplier shall confirm that the Screening Checks have been completed (including Screening Checks on any previous or alternate names by which the Supplier Deployed Personnel are commonly known) and report the outcome of Screening Checks to the Customer using the relevant Customer Group country HR management reporting system or process requested by the Customer.  This report must confirm that there have been no Adverse Findings, or must set out any Adverse Findings in detail.  2.8  The Supplier shall ensure that the Supplier Deployed Personnel:  (a)  provide their consent in writing to the Supplier to carry out the Screening Checks and report the outcome of Screening Checks to the Customer;  (b)  complete the Screening Checks in full;  (c)  complete and sign the Self Certification Declaration;  (d)  complete and sign the Confidentiality Undertaking;  (e)  have been notified of the Customer Group Non Employed Workers Privacy Statement.  The Supplier shall retain the relevant written consents, Self Certification Declaration and Confidentiality Undertaking for the purposes of Screening Verification.  2.9  If the Supplier is unable to carry out any of the required Screening Checks, upon written request, the Customer may provide such Screening Checks requested by the Supplier using the Customer Service Provider(s) on the Supplier's behalf. In order to carry out such Screening Checks, the Supplier will be required to provide:  (a)  its consent in writing for the Customer to provide such Screening Checks; and  (b)  procure the consent in writing of the Supplier Deployed Personnel to be screened by the Customer; and  (c)  provide the Customer with any other necessary information and documents in the form required by the Customer to perform the Screening Checks on behalf of the Supplier in accordance with Applicable Laws.  2.10  The Supplier will ensure that the Supplier Deployed Personnel undertake such mandatory training as may be required by the Customer to comply with any Applicable Laws at the Customer's cost with respect to his/her role and responsibilities for the services undertaken within the Customer Group.  2.11  Upon request, the Supplier shall provide verification evidence to the Customer that it has completed the Screening Checks and has complied with the Customer Group Screening Check Standards and shall provide the original signed Confidentiality Undertaking requested by the Customer ('Screening Verification').  2.12  Upon request, the Supplier will supply to the Customer, its internal and external auditors and/or a Regulatory Authority any other records or information required by Applicable Laws related to the Screening Checks carried out in relation to the Contract Document.  Vendor Management of Supplier Deployed Personnel  2.13  The Customer may contract with the Customer Service Providers to support the engagement process for on boarding of Supplier Deployed Personnel across the Customer Group. The Supplier agrees to co operate with the Customer and such Customer Service Providers as reasonably requested by the Customer from time to time in relation to the management of the Supplier Deployed Personnel population using the relevant Vendor Management System. The Vendor Management System is made available by the Customer and its third party licensors on an 'as is' and an 'as available' basis. Such co operation request shall include entering required information relating to the Supplied Deployed Personnel into the relevant Vendor Management System.  2.14  The Customer grants the Supplier the royalty free right to use the Vendor Management System, subject to the Terms of Use and availability of the VMS.  2.15  The Customer and the Supplier shall comply with applicable Data Protection Laws in relation to the processing of Personal Data of Supplier Deployed Personnel. Any Confidential Information hosted in the VMS shall be subject to the obligations of confidentiality under the Contract Document. The Customer Group may process Personal Data of Supplier Deployed Personnel for purposes related to the Contract Document and for relevant purposes in accordance with the Customer Group Non Employed Workers Privacy Statement. For such purposes, the Customer may transfer such Personal Data to any country where the Customer Group operates. The Customer shall provide reasonable assistance to the Supplier in respect of any data subject information requests received by the Supplier from the Supplier Deployed Personnel relating to the Customer's processing of their Personal Data. The Customer reserves the right to charge for such assistance where the costs of such assistance become significant.

clauses.description: 15. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION  15.1 Except as otherwise expressly provided in the Contract Document, a Party shall (and shall ensure that its Personnel and professional advisers shall) not at any time disclose or make available to any person or use, copy, adapt or alter for any purpose, any Confidential Information obtained by it ('Recipient Party') from the other Party ('Disclosing Party') without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.  15.2 The Recipient Party shall treat and keep Confidential Information confidential and take all necessary precautions to ensure that all Confidential Information is held in confidence (whether communicated orally, in writing, electronic form or otherwise) and minimise the risk of unauthorised disclosure or use of such Confidential Information.  15.3 No Party will be under an obligation to keep Confidential Information confidential that it can demonstrate:  15.3.1 is publicly available or generally known to the public at the time of disclosure or has subsequently entered into the public domain, except as a result of a breach of the Contract Document;  15.3.2 was already in the Recipient Party's possession from another source prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party and the Recipient Party was not under any obligation of confidentiality in respect of the Confidential Information when it was received from the other source not connected to a Party under the Contract Document; or  15.3.3 is independently developed by the Recipient Party.  15.4 The following circumstances shall not constitute a breach of the obligations of confidence set out in this clause 15 by the Recipient Party where Confidential Information is disclosed:  15.4.1 to the Recipient Party's Personnel and professional advisers to the extent necessary to enable the Recipient Party to exercise its rights and perform its obligations under the Contract Document, provided that prior to disclosure such Personnel and professional advisers have entered into an appropriate written confidentiality undertaking or non disclosure agreement with the Recipient Party to treat and keep such Confidential Information confidential on terms no less onerous than contained in the Contract Document;  15.4.2 where necessary to members of the Customer Group (and their Personnel and professional advisers):  (A) for the purposes of the Contract Document; or  (B) for the proper exercise of any licences granted to the Customer and any member of the Customer Group under the Contract Document,  subject to the Customer procuring that each Customer Group member (and its Personnel and professional advisers) treat and keep such Confidential Information confidential;  15.4.3 to the Customer's Auditors, for the purposes of the audit provisions set out in the Regulatory Schedule and elsewhere in the Contract Document, subject to the Customer procuring that each Auditor shall treat and keep such Confidential Information confidential;  15.4.4 to Customer Service Providers, to the extent necessary to enable those Customer Service Providers to provide or offer to provide goods or services to the Customer or other Customer Group members, subject to the Customer procuring that any Customer Service Providers will treat and keep such Confidential Information confidential and shall use such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of providing the Supplies to the Customer or Customer Group members under the Order Form; and  15.4.5 if and to the extent it is required to do so by any law or regulation or by any court, Regulatory Authority or government agency or authority, provided that to the extent that it is permitted to do so under Applicable Law, the Recipient Party:  (A) provides the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request; and  (B) co operates with the Disclosing Party and uses all reasonable endeavours to avoid or limit disclosure and to obtain any necessary confidentiality undertakings relating to the onward disclosure from the intended recipient.  15.5 Upon the Customer's reasonable request or where required by a Regulatory Authority, the Supplier shall obtain from its Personnel and professional advisers a written confidentiality undertaking or non disclosure agreement in favour of the Customer in a form approved by the Customer containing confidentiality obligations no less onerous than those contained in the Contract Document prior to receiving or accessing any Customer Confidential Information.  15.6 The Customer shall retain all rights in, title to and interest in and to the Customer Confidential Information.  15.7 Where the Customer makes Confidential Information available to the Supplier belonging to a Third Party, the Supplier shall, if requested to do so by the Customer, enter into an appropriate confidentiality undertaking or non disclosure agreement with that Third Party prior to receipt of such Confidential Information.  15.8 Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies that the Parties may have, the Parties agree that damages may not be an adequate remedy for any breach of this clause 15 and that the Parties shall be entitled to seek the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of this clause 15.  15.9 The Supplier shall upon the Customer's request, or as soon as such retention is no longer required for the performance of the Supplier's obligations under the Contract Document, delete, destroy or return the Customer Confidential Information and storage media in accordance with a process approved by the Customer. Upon the Customer's request, the Supplier shall certify in writing that deletion or destruction has taken place. Such deletion or destruction must be carried out in an appropriate and auditable manner that ensures that the Customer Confidential Information cannot be re created, accessed or read, including:  15.9.1 for electronic media that will be redeployed, a sanitation process that deletes data before redeployment, which destroys boot partitions, file pointers, and user data, and prevents all data from being reconstructed and read, in accordance with Good Industry Practice guidelines, for example, as set out in National Institute of Standards Technology (NIST) SP800 88 or US Department of Defence (DoD) 5220.22 M Directives; and  15.9.2 for disposal of documents, by cross shredding or using a secure waste disposal process designated for the Customer Confidential Information.
