or (iv) or would otherwise be unethical, illegal or improper;  2.1.2 is and shall remain compliant with all anti bribery and anti corruption laws and regulations: (i) in the country or countries in which Supplies are provided; and (ii) otherwise applicable to the Supplier;  2.1.3 shall not knowingly do or omit to do anything that may cause the Customer to be in breach of applicable anti bribery or anti corruption laws, including but not limited to the United Kingdom Bribery Act 2010 and United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act 1977; and  2.1.4 shall, unless otherwise prohibited by applicable law, disclose to the Customer all details of any non compliance with paragraphs 2.1.1, 2.1.2 and 2.1.3 of this Schedule, and any non compliance by any other person envisaged by paragraph 2.2 immediately, upon becoming aware of such non compliance.  2.2 If the Supplier subcontracts to another person any right or obligation under the Contract Document or has another person perform any act or not perform any act in relation to the Contract Document, the Supplier shall procure such other person to comply with paragraph 2 of this Schedule or clauses materially equivalent, such that all forms of bribery and corruption are expressly prohibited, and the Supplier shall remain liable to the Customer for the performance by such person of such obligations as permissible under relevant law. For the purposes of this paragraph, a 'person' is any individual, partnership, company or any other legal entity, public or private.  2.3 It is a condition of the Contract Document that the Supplier complies with paragraphs 2.1 and 2.2 of this Schedule. If the Supplier breaches, or the Customer reasonably believes that the Supplier has breached, paragraph 2.1 or paragraph 2.2, without prejudice to any other remedy available to the Customer, the Customer may terminate the Contract Document upon written notice to the Supplier, and the Supplier shall not be entitled to receive any compensation in respect of such termination.  2.4 For the purpose of this paragraph 2, the following capitalised terms shall have the following meanings:  Politically Exposed Persons means: (i) an individual who is or has, at any time, been entrusted with prominent public functions; or (ii) an immediate family member, or known close associate of the individual entrusted with a prominent public function.   Public Official means:  (i) any officer, employee or representative of a government, at any level of seniority, whether national, federal or local (for example, regulators, legislators, customs officials, central bank governors, ministers, and tribal authorities);   (ii) any individual exercising a legislative, administrative, regulatory, judicial or other public or official function, whether appointed or elected;   (iii) any officer, employee or representative, at any level, of a State Owned Entity;  (iv) members of royal families;   (v) representatives of political parties and political candidates;   (vi) any officer, employee or representative of a public international organisation (for example the EU, World Bank, IMF and UN);  (vii) private individuals or entities contracted by a State Owned Entity or the government to exercise a public function;   (viii) Politically Exposed Persons; and  (ix) any other parties determined to be a 'sovereign', 'public official', 'government official', or the like, under local law.  State Owned Entity means any government or state owned or controlled entity, instrumentality or department where the government or state:  (i) has 50% or more ownership,   (ii) board or voting control, or  (iii) is otherwise deemed to have a golden share, or the power to veto key shareholder decisions.  Examples include, but are not limited to: sovereign wealth funds, state controlled utilities and hospitals, pension funds, and central banks.  An entity does not cease to be a State Owned Entity if it purports to act in a private capacity (e.g. a public company traded on a securities exchange) or by the fact that services are rendered without compensation.  [OPTION B: LONG FORM TEMPLATE CLAUSE  The long form ABC clause set out below is for inclusion in Contracts with Third Parties that are high risk Intermediaries or engages a high risk Intermediary; or the Intermediary is based in a VERY HIGH RISK Country or the vendor contract is for a VERY HIGH RISK country with a cumulative vendor spend that is equal to, or greater than, USD 50,000.  Currently the VERY HIGH RISK Countries for which the long form ABC clause must always be included are Iraq, Bangladesh, Cambodia, Myanmar and Nigeria. Any issues with including the Long Form ABC Clause for VERY HIGH RISK Countries should be taken up with ABC COE via Ask Compliance .]  3. ANTI BRIBERY AND CORRUPTION  3.1 The Supplier represents, warrants and undertakes that:  3.1.1 it and each Supplier Group Company has not and shall not (and shall ensure to the fullest extent possible that its employees and agents and others under its direction or control do not) make or offer directly or indirectly any payment, gift or other advantage which: (i) would violate any applicable anti bribery or anti corruption laws including but not limited to the United Kingdom Bribery Act 2010 and United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act 1977; (ii) is intended to, or does, influence or reward any person for acting in breach of an expectation of good faith, impartiality or trust, or would otherwise be improper for the recipient to accept; (iii) is made to or for a Public Official with the intention of influencing them to obtain or retain an advantage in the conduct of business; or (iv) would otherwise be unethical, illegal or improper, (any one of (i) to (iv) being a 'Corrupt Act');  3.1.2 it and each Supplier Group Company is and shall remain compliant with all anti bribery and anti corruption laws and regulations: (i) in the country or countries in which the Supplies are provided; and (ii) otherwise applicable to the Supplier (or the relevant Supplier Group Company);  3.1.3 it and each Supplier Group Company shall not knowingly do or omit to do anything that may cause the Customer to be in breach of applicable anti bribery or anti corruption laws including but not limited to the United Kingdom Bribery Act 2010 and United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act 1977;  3.1.4 it and each Supplier Group Company and to the best of its knowledge, each relevant Connected Person acting on behalf of the Supplier has not been found by a court or tribunal to have committed a Corrupt Act;  3.1.5 to the best of its knowledge, no Public Official or a Politically Exposed Person has any direct interest in any payments made by the Customer under the Contract Document; and  3.1.6 it and each Supplier Group Company shall maintain and enforce anti bribery and corruption policies and procedures designed to:  (A) prevent bribery;  (B) promote accepted standards of business conduct and ethics; and  (C) prevent any Connected Persons from undertaking conduct that would constitute a Corrupt Act.  3.2 The Supplier shall undertake such anti corruption training as the Customer may reasonably require.  3.3 The Supplier accepts that the Customer is relying upon each of the representations and warranties provided in paragraph 2.1 (the 'ABC Warranties') and each of the ABC Warranties shall be construed as being separate and independent, and continuing in nature.  3.4 If the Supplier engages a Subcontractor or has another person perform any of its obligation, the Supplier shall procure such other person to comply with paragraph 2.1 and include clauses materially equivalent in the written subcontract and employment contracts in relation to prohibition of Corrupt Acts, and the Supplier shall remain liable to the Customer for the performance by such person of such obligations as permissible under relevant law. For the purposes of this paragraph, a 'person' is any individual, partnership, company or any other legal entity, public or private.  3.5 The Supplier shall provide all assistance and information reasonably requested by the Customer, to enable the Customer to comply with applicable anti bribery and corruption laws, in particular, the Supplier shall:  3.5.1 maintain comprehensive and accurate records and supporting documentation in relation to the Supplies provided and the Connected Persons acting on behalf of the Supplier or any other Supplier Group Company, and retain such records and supporting documentation as required under applicable laws; and  3.5.2 give the Customer access, on reasonable notice, to inspect and audit the premises, staff, equipment, records, supporting documents, and systems relating to the Supplies, and at the Customer's request make all reasonable changes required pursuant to such audit or inspection. If the action comprises the correction of a defect in the Supplies or the manner in which such Supplies are provided, the Supplier shall implement the action at no additional charge to the Customer.    3.6 Unless otherwise prohibited in law, the Supplier shall notify the Customer immediately upon becoming aware of:  3.6.1 any breaches of paragraph 2.1 (and any non compliance by any other person envisaged by paragraph 2.4);  3.6.2 any changes in circumstances, knowledge or awareness during the contract term relating to the Supplier's ability to continue to comply with the representations and warranties set out in paragraph 2.1, including but not limited to requests or demands for bribes.  3.7 Without prejudice to any other remedy available to it, the Customer may terminate this Contract Document at its sole discretion for breach if the Supplier breaches, or the Customer believes that the Supplier has breached paragraph 2.1 upon providing the Supplier with written notice, and the Supplier shall not be entitled to receive any compensation or other remuneration in respect of such termination.  3.8 For the purpose of this paragraph 2, the following capitalised terms shall have the following meanings:  Connected Persons means any employees, consultants, subcontractors, directors, officers, shareholders, representatives or agents of the Supplier [(or any other Supplier Group Company)] and, to the extent not included in the foregoing, any Supplier Associated Persons.  Corrupt Act has the meaning given to it in paragraph 3.1.  Politically Exposed Persons means: (i) an individual who is or has, at any time, been entrusted with prominent public functions; or (ii) an immediate family member or known close associate of the individual entrusted with a prominent public function.   Public Official means:  (i) any officer, employee or representative of a government, at any level of seniority, whether national, federal or local (for example, regulators, legislators, customs officials, central bank governors, ministers, and tribal authorities);   (ii) any individual exercising a legislative, administrative, regulatory, judicial or other public or official function, whether appointed or elected;   (iii) any officer, employee or representative, at any level, of a State Owned Entity;  (iv) members of royal families;   (v) representatives of political parties and political candidates;   (vi) any officer, employee or representative of a public international organisation (for example the EU, World Bank, IMF and UN);  (vii) private individuals or entities contracted by a State Owned Entity or the government to exercise a public function;   (viii) Politically Exposed Persons; and  (ix) any other parties determined to be a 'sovereign', 'public official', 'government official', or the like, under local law.   State Owned Entity means any government or state owned or controlled entity, instrumentality or department where the government or state:  (i) has 50% or more ownership,   (ii) board or voting control, or  (iii) is otherwise deemed to have a golden share, or the power to veto key shareholder decisions.  Examples include, but are not limited to: sovereign wealth funds, state controlled utilities and hospitals, pension funds, and central banks. An entity does not cease to be a State Owned Entity if it purports to act in a private capacity (e.g. a public company traded on a securities exchange) or by the fact that services are rendered without compensation.  Supplier Associated Person means, a person who performs services, for or on behalf of the Supplier [(or any other Supplier Group Company)]. This includes all staff and will generally include suppliers of services, outsourced service providers, intermediaries (including 'senior advisors'), agents, distributors and may include subsidiaries, joint ventures and consortia.  Supplier Group Company means the Supplier, and  (i) any person, body corporate, partnership, firm or other entity in which on or after the date of the Contract Document from time to time the Supplier directly or indirectly:  (a) owns more than half the capital, income, business assets or any other comparable equity or ownership interest; or   (b) has the power to exercise fifty percent (50%) or more of the voting rights in such entity; or   (c) has the legal power to direct or cause the direction or general management or affairs of the entity in question; or   (d) has the power to appoint or remove more than half the members of the supervisory board, board of directors or bodies legally representing such entity; or   (e) has the right to manage the business of such entity;    (ii) any person, body corporate, partnership, firm or other entity which directly or indirectly has the rights or powers listed in (i) above in or over the Supplier ('Supplier Controller'); and  (iii) any person, body corporate, partnership, firm or other entity in which a Supplier Controller directly or indirectly has the rights to powers listed in (i) above.

clauses.description: 14. CONTINGENCY PLANNING (BUSINESS CONTINUITY AND CRISIS MANAGEMENT)  The following capitalised terms shall have the meanings given to them below.  Business Continuity Plan has the meaning given to it in paragraph 14.2.  Business Continuity Policy has the meaning given to it in paragraph 14.1.  Crisis has the meaning given to it in paragraph 14.11.  Crisis Management Plan has the meaning given to it in paragraph 14.13.  Crisis Management Policy has the meaning given to it in paragraph 14.12.  Crisis Management Team has the meaning given to it in paragraph 14.1214.13.  IBS has the meaning given to it in this paragraph 14.  Business Continuity Requirements  14.1 The Supplier shall have in place a formal business continuity policy (the 'Business Continuity Policy') that delivers plans for the continuation of Supplies without interruption.  14.2 The Supplier shall define and maintain a comprehensive business continuity plan including documented arrangements that define how the Supplier will respond, recover, resume and restore to a pre defined and acceptable level of operations following a business disruption (the 'Business Continuity Plan'). The Business Continuity Plan must:  14.2.1 outline the major foreseeable operational disruption scenarios which may lead to the need to invoke the Business Continuity Plan, including:  (A) unavailability of critical buildings and their susceptibility to threats (e.g., location in a flood plain) including simultaneous threats to multiple locations, potential concentration risk and the proximity to critical infrastructure (e.g. power grids, telecommunications, nuclear power plants, airports, major highways, and railroads);  (B) unavailability of critical staff in any scenario (e.g. epidemic/pandemic, loss of building from climate related physical risk events, and transport disruption);  (C) unavailability of technology and systems required to perform services upon which the Supplies to the Customer are dependent;  (D) unavailability of critical data or information (e.g. malware attack); and  (E) unavailability of alternative fourth party suppliers and/or the capability to assume or bring back 'in house' sub contractual arrangements upon which the Supplies to the Customer are dependent;  14.2.2 detail service and business continuity procedures and plans, and contingency alternatives (including priorities to be accorded and resources to be allocated) designed to minimise the impact of any operational disruption to the provision of the Supplies in accordance with the Contract Document;  14.2.3 describe the Customer's, the Supplier's and Subcontractor's specific responsibilities and dependencies upon invocation of the Business Continuity Plan, and specify lines of communication between the Supplier and the Customer in such circumstances;  14.2.4 specify recovery time objectives and the Supplier's liability for not achieving specified recovery time objectives; and  14.2.5 be consistent with all Site Standards and Procedures and Customer policies.  14.3 The Business Continuity Plan shall be provided to the Customer for comment prior to the commencement of the Contract Document. The Supplier shall make any modifications to the Business Continuity Plan as reasonably requested by the Customer.  Business Continuity Acceptance and Amendment Procedures    14.4 The Customer shall, within ten (10) Business Days after receiving the Supplier's Business Continuity Plan, provide a written response to the Supplier indicating whether the Customer accepts, rejects, or proposes a modification to any aspect of the Business Continuity Plan.  14.5 If the Customer requests a modification to the Business Continuity Plan provided by the Supplier, then the Parties shall promptly meet and act in good faith to agree in writing on variations to the Business Continuity Plan which are acceptable to the Customer.  14.6 If the Customer rejects the Business Continuity Plan provided by the Supplier, the Supplier shall submit to the Customer a revised Business Continuity Plan no later than five (5) Business Days after receipt of notice of rejection or modification (as applicable) from the Customer, and the acceptance and amendment process shall start again until the Business Continuity Plan is approved by the Customer.]  Business Continuity Annual Review and Testing  14.7 The Supplier shall:  14.7.1 review and update the Business Continuity Plan at least on an annual basis so as to ensure that it remains operational, provide a revised copy to the Customer for comment within ten (10) Business Days after each update and make any modifications to the Business Continuity Plan as reasonably requested by the Customer;  14.7.2 test the Business Continuity Plan annually, including the documented procedures for invocation of the Business Continuity Plan at least annually;  14.7.3 share the results of testing with the Customer on request or when any issues are identified during testing;  14.7.4 participate in joint testing and exercises with the Customer at mutually agreed intervals;  14.7.5 undertake on the Customer's request a walkthrough of the Business Continuity Plan with the Customer to validate the Business Continuity Plan and the associated procedures that invoke the Business Continuity Plan; and  14.7.6 as part of the Supplies onboarding process, and thereafter on the Customer's request, provide an attestation confirming that the Business Continuity Plan is sufficient to enable the continuity of the Supplies without material interruption for the duration of the Contract Document.  : The Customer shall take such reasonable steps to support the Supplier's testing of each Business Continuity Plan.]  : The Customer shall implement and test its business continuity and contingency plans in accordance with the Customer policies. The Supplier shall, on request from the Customer, provide such assistance and support as requested by the Customer in relation to the testing of the Customer's business continuity and contingency plans]  : , including without limitation:  (A) [insert specific assistance and support required by the Customer (if any); and]  (B) [insert specific assistance and support required by the Customer (if any)].]  Business Continuity Implementation and Communication  14.8 The Supplier shall implement the applicable Business Continuity Plan in the event of an operational disruption and shall comply with its obligations under the Business Continuity Plan.  14.9 The Supplier shall immediately notify the Customer:  14.9.1 upon becoming aware of any operational disruption or other adverse event or circumstances that may significantly affect the ability of the Supplier to provide the Supplies in accordance with the Contract Document;  14.9.2 of any adverse event for which the Supplier has invoked or may be required to invoke its Business Continuity Plan; and  14.9.3 of any other material changes to its Business Continuity Policy and/or Business Continuity Plan.  14.10 If an operational disruption continues to prevent, hinder or delay performance of the Supplies for more than ten (10) Business Days following the commencement of the operational disruption, the Customer may terminate the Contract Document (or part thereof) immediately by written notice to the Supplier.  Crisis Management Requirements  14.11 A 'Crisis' is defined as an abnormal and unstable condition that threatens the organisation's strategic objectives, reputation, or viability.  14.12 The Supplier shall have in place a formal crisis management policy, (the 'Crisis Management Policy') that details the Supplier's process and plans for responding to Crisis events and emergencies.  14.13 The Supplier shall define and maintain a comprehensive crisis management plan including the assemblage of the 'Crisis Management Team' (to be defined in the Crisis Management Plan), situation assessment, disaster declaration procedure and notification processes, evacuation procedure, resource mobilisation for recovery, and establishment of command and control to facilitate recovery (the 'Crisis Management Plan').  14.14 The Crisis Management Plan shall be provided to the Customer for comment prior to the commencement of the Contract Document. The Supplier shall make any modifications to the Crisis Management Plan as reasonably requested by the Customer.  Crisis Management Acceptance and Amendment Procedures   14.15 The Customer shall, within ten (10) Business Days after receiving the Supplier's Crisis Management Plan, provide a written response to the Supplier indicating whether the Customer accepts, rejects, or proposes a modification to any aspect of the Crisis Management Plan.  14.16 If the Customer requests a modification to the Crisis Management Plan provided by the Supplier, then the Parties shall promptly meet and act in good faith to agree in writing on variations to the Crisis Management Plan which are acceptable to the Customer.  14.17 If the Customer rejects the Crisis Management Plan provided by the Supplier, the Supplier shall submit to the Customer a revised Crisis Management Plan no later than five (5) Business Days after receipt of notice of rejection or modification (as applicable) from the Customer, and the acceptance and amendment process shall start again until the Crisis Management Plan is approved by the Customer.]  Crisis Management Annual Review and Testing  14.18 The Supplier shall:  14.18.1 review and update the Crisis Management Plan at least on an annual basis or when major operational changes occur so as to ensure that it remains operational, provide a revised copy to the Customer for comment within ten (10) Business Days after each update and make any modifications to the Crisis Management Plan as reasonably requested by the Customer;  14.18.2 test the Crisis Management Plan, including the documented procedures for invocation of the Crisis Management Plan, at least annually;  14.18.3 share the results of testing with the Customer on request or when any issues are identified during testing;  14.18.4 participate in joint testing and exercises with the Customer at mutually agreed intervals;  14.18.5 undertake on the Customer's request a walkthrough of the Crisis Management Plan with the Customer to validate the Crisis Management Plan and the associated procedures which invoke the Crisis Management Plan; and  14.18.6 as part of the Supplies onboarding process, and thereafter on the Customer's request, provide an attestation confirming that the Crisis Management Plan is sufficient to enable the continuity of the Supplies without material interruption for the duration of the Contract Document.  The Customer shall take such reasonable steps to support the Supplier's testing of each Crisis Management Plan.]  The Customer shall implement and test its Crisis Management Plans in accordance with the Customer policies. The Supplier shall, on request from the Customer, provide such assistance and support as requested by the Customer in relation to the testing of the Customer's Crisis Management Plans.]  , including without limitation:  [insert specific assistance and support required by the Customer (if any)]; and  [insert specific assistance and support required by the Customer (if any)].]  Crisis Management Implementation and Continuation  14.19 The Supplier shall implement the applicable Crisis Management Plan in the event of a Crisis and shall comply with its obligations under the Crisis Management Plan.  14.20 The Supplier shall immediately notify the Customer:  14.20.1 upon becoming aware of any Crisis or other adverse event or circumstances that may significantly affect the ability of the Supplier to provide the Supplies in accordance with the Contract Document;  14.20.2 of any adverse event for which the Supplier has invoked or may be required to invoke its Crisis Management Plan; and  14.20.3 of any other material changes to its Crisis Management Policy and/or Crisis Management Plan.  14.21 If a Crisis continues to prevent, hinder or delay performance of the Supplies for more than ten (10) Business Days following the commencement of the Crisis, the Customer may terminate the Contract Document (or part thereof) immediately by written notice to the Supplier.  [DRAFTING NOTE: THE FOLLOWING PARAGRAPHS 14.22 TO 14.23 ON IBS TO BE INSERTED ONLY FOR TRANSACTIONS USING THE FOLLOWING SUPPLY SCHEDULES: (1) BUSINESS PROCESS OUTSOURCING SCHEDULE; (2) CONSULTANCY SERVICES SCHEDULE; (3) GOODS AND SERVICES SCHEDULE; AND (4) GENERAL SERVICES SCHEDULE. IT CAN BE REMOVED FOR ALL OTHER TRANSACTIONS.]  Operational Resilience Requirements for Important Business Services ('IBS')  14.22 In addition to the business continuity and crisis management requirements stated above, if the Customer advises the Supplier that the Supplies provided by the Supplier integrally support the delivery of IBS, the Supplier shall:  14.22.1 ensure the level of service provided during severe but plausible disruption is aligned with the impact tolerance of the Customer (as communicated to the Supplier by the Customer) for the respective IBS; and  14.22.2 at the Customer's reasonable request, participate in scenario testing exercises conducted by the Customer to test end to end IBS for its ability to remain within the impact tolerance of the Customer during severe but plausible disruption, without limiting any Supplier obligations under the Contract Document to meet or exceed any applicable Service Levels in the performance of the Services.  14.23 If the Supplier is unable to comply with paragraph 14.22.1 then the Customer shall be entitled to terminate the Contract Document with immediate effect.

clauses.description: 14. IT DISASTER RECOVERY  14.1 [Unless expressly set out in the Order Form, the Recovery Time Objective ('RTO') for the Services is [insert number of hours] hours and Recovery Point Objective ('RPO') for the Services is [insert number of hours] hours.   14.2 The Supplier shall ensure that:  14.2.1 the Disaster Recovery Plan is consistent with other enterprise management disciplines such as Crisis Management Plan and Business Continuity Plans;  14.2.2 the Disaster Recovery Plan follows the Customer's standard template as notified by the Customer from time to time (where applicable);  14.2.3 the recovery procedures cater to partial failure scenarios limiting outage impact;  14.2.4 the dependency analysis is performed to establish linkages between crucial business functions and recovery capabilities within the Services to facilitate recovery;  14.2.5 the Disaster Recovery Plan is capable of meeting the RTO and RPO set out in clause 14.1 or the Order Form;  14.2.6 recovery capabilities are consistent with and prioritised according to the criticality of the Services and the Service Levels;  14.2.7 it adopts the Customer Group's ITSCM Continuity Management Framework and Recovery Management Procedures to govern implementation of the Services and disaster recovery testing;  14.2.8 the Services are tested in the Supplier's disaster recovery test environment in line with the Customer Group's disaster recovery test roadmap and at least once every six (6) months ('Disaster Recovery Test'); and  14.2.9 all issues identified as part of a Disaster Recovery Test are resolved with detailed analysis of the root cause of such issues and recommendations as to how such issues can be remedied and not repeated. The Supplier shall share its root cause analysis report with the Customer on request.  14.3 In the event that the Disaster Recovery Plan is invoked in accordance with the Disaster Recovery Plan, where business continuity is at risk or recovery of the Services cannot otherwise be achieved, the Supplier shall migrate the Services and content to the disaster recovery facility(s) in accordance with the Disaster Recovery Plan.  14.4 [If the Services are unable to meet the RTO and RPO set out in clause 14.1 or the Order Form over [insert number] Disaster Recovery Tests, then, in addition to all other remedies available to the Customer under the Contract Document or Applicable Laws, the Customer may terminate the Contract Document on written notice to the Supplier with no liability, obligation or penalty to the Customer by reason of such termination.]]

clauses.description: 26.  DIVESTED BUSINESS  26.1  The Customer may, from time to time, restructure, sell or transfer a member of the Customer Group or a department or division within a member of the Customer Group (a 'Divestment' with the sold, transferred or restructured Customer Group member, or part of the Customer Group member, becoming a 'Divested Business').   26.2  Pursuant to a Divestment, the Customer may:  26.2.1  require the Supplier to continue to provide Supplies to the Divested Business in accordance with the Contract Document, as though the Divestment has not occurred, and the Customer shall pay the Charges for such Supplies provided to the Divested Business. Accordingly, the Divested Business will continue to enjoy all rights and benefits it is entitled to as if it were still a Customer Group member, which right may continue for up to eighteen (18) months following the effective date of the relevant Divestment, provided that whilst the Customer shall continue to be able to recover Losses suffered by the Divested Business, the Divested Business shall not itself be entitled to enforce the Contract Document against the Supplier or Supplier's Affiliate and any claim by the Supplier against the Divested Business in relation to the Contract Document shall be brought against the Customer; or   26.2.2  partially assign or novate the benefits or rights to receive the Supplies, in which event the Supplier will, or will procure that a Supplier's Affiliate will, enter into a separate contract ('Divestment Agreement') with the Divested Business on no less favourable terms than those set out in the Contract Document.

clauses.description: 10. RECOVERY AND RESOLUTION PLANNING ('RRP')  10.1 The Supplier acknowledges that the Customer or any other member of the Customer Group (together with the Customer, a 'Customer Group Member') may upon occasion be required to: (a) restructure, amalgamate, reorganise, wind down and/or divest all or any part of the business, shares, assets or liabilities of any entity within the Customer Group in relation to, in preparation for, or as a consequence of any Resolution or the execution of any recovery plan maintained by that entity (each such action being a 'Restructure'); and (b) procure that such entity, its successor or assign, or product of such Restructure or its successor (each a 'Restructured Entity') will be able to operate independently of the Customer Group.  10.2 Unless the context otherwise requires, capitalised terms used but not defined in this Schedule shall have the meaning given to them in the Contract Document. For the purposes of this paragraph:  10.2.1 'Restructure' and 'Restructured Entity' each has the meaning given in paragraph 10.1;  10.2.2 'Resolution' means the exercise by a Resolution Authority of any of its Resolution Powers in relation to any Customer Group Member or the Restructured Entity under:  (A) the United Kingdom ('UK') Banking Act 2009 (as amended) or any other future legislative measures extending to financial institutions or their affiliates; or  (B) equivalent resolution related legislation applicable to non UK parties.  10.2.3 'Resolution Authority' means in relation to any Customer Group Member or the Restructured Entity authority, including (but not limited to):  (A) the Bank of England and any resolution administrator appointed by the Bank of England; and  (B) any central bank, government department or agency in any part of the world,  which is empowered to exercise Resolution Powers.  10.2.4 'Resolution Powers'  means any statutory power exercisable by an authority allowing it to take steps to resolve any Customer Group Member or to otherwise take steps pursuant to its statutory powers to facilitate the recovery of the Customer Group Member from any financial difficulty (including, for the avoidance of doubt, any steps taken by the Bank of England to restrict the exercise of any contractual rights contained in any agreements to which the Customer Group Member is party).  10.3 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the Contract Document, in the event of any Resolution in respect of a Customer Group Member or deterioration in the financial position of any Customer Group Member, for so long as the agreed Charges under the Contract Document have been paid when due (subject to any grace period provided for under the Contract Document), the Supplier for no extra charge:  10.3.1 shall continue providing the Supplies (or the relevant part thereof) to or for the benefit of any Customer Group Member and/or the Restructured Entity and permitting such  Customer Group Member and/or Restructured Entity to use such Supplies in the manner agreed in the Contract Document and as if there has been no Restructure. Where a Charge is payable in respect of a part of the Supplies only, the Supplier will make a fair and equitable adjustment of the agreed Charges that would be payable for such part;  10.3.2 where appropriate, consents to any Customer Group Member providing the Restructured Entity with the relevant Supplies (or the relevant part thereof);  10.3.3 consents to any Customer Group Member assigning or novating the Contract Document and/or any of the licenses and other rights granted under the Contract Document, in whole or in part to the Restructured Entity or its new owner (and shall execute any document or agreement necessary to give effect to such assignment or novation);  10.3.4 shall, upon any Customer Group Member's request, enter into an agreement (a 'Separate Contract') with the Restructured Entity for the provision of the Supplies (or the relevant part thereof) to the Restructured Entity or its new owner, on the same terms and conditions as the Contract Document, except as amended to:  (A) comply with any orders or directions of the Resolution Authority or a court or the applicable law;  (B) reflect the separation of liability of the Restructured Entity under the Separate Contract from any liability of a Customer Group Member under the Contract Document to the extent that the Contract Document continues; and  (c) reflect any changes to the scope of the Supplies to be provided to the Restructured Entity or its new owner, where the relevant Customer Group Member (acting in its sole discretion) determines that the original scope of Supplies may not be appropriate following a Restructure;  10.3.5 shall facilitate, without interruption or disruption to any Customer Group Member's business, the transfer of the Supplies (or the relevant part thereof) to the Restructured Entity or its new owner following an assignment, novation or entry into of a Separate Contract pursuant to paragraphs 10.3.3 and 10.3.4 above; and  10.3.6 agrees that, for so long as any Customer Group Member remains subject to a deterioration in its financial circumstances and/or Resolution, no right of the Supplier to terminate, materially vary or suspend the provision of the Supplies and/or the performance of its obligations under the Contract Document shall be deemed to arise.  10.4 If a Restructure results in a Restructured Entity no longer being part of the Customer Group, the Supplier shall comply with 10.3 above (as relevant from time to time), for such a period as the Customer may reasonably require.

clauses.description: 5.  CFA COMPLIANCE/ ANTI TAX EVASION  5.1  The Supplier will, and will use best endeavours to procure that its officers, employees, agents, Subcontractors and any other persons who perform services for or on its behalf in connection with the Contract Document or in relation to any matters which are the subject of the Contract Document will:  5.1.1  not do or omit to do any act or thing which constitutes or may constitute a Tax Evasion    Offence; and/or  5.1.2  not do or omit to do any act or thing which causes or may cause the Customer to commit a Tax Evasion Offence;   5.2  The Supplier will immediately give written notice to the Customer upon a breach, or suspected breach, of any of its obligations at paragraph 5.1 occurring or upon gaining knowledge of a breach, or suspected breach by any of its officers, employees, agents, Subcontractors and/or any other persons who perform services for or on the Supplier's behalf in connection with the Contract Document or in relation to any matters which are the subject of the Contract Document and the Customer will be entitled by giving written notice to that effect to the Supplier, to require the Supplier to remove from the performance of the Contract Document any of the above mentioned persons/ parties who perform services for or on behalf of the Supplier in connection with the Contract Document and in respect of whom the Supplier is in breach of any of its obligations under paragraph 5.1.

clauses.clause_key: Release Management and Change Management Services@Software Maintenance Schedule
clauses.description: 7.  NEW RELEASES  7.1  The Supplier shall:  7.1.1  keep the Customer fully informed of any modification, enhancement or addition to, or new version or replacement of, the Maintained Software, (including new features, functions, technology or architecture updates) ('New Releases'); and  7.1.2  upon request, consult with and provide any information reasonably requested by the Customer in relation to the New Releases, including any Documentation relating to the New Releases.  7.2  The Supplier shall offer to the Customer any New Release that becomes available immediately upon such New Release becoming available.  7.3  If the Customer notifies the Supplier that it wishes to Use a New Release the Supplier shall, at the Customer's direction and a time to be agreed by the Party's Representatives, deliver, install, integrate and test (and provide the Customer with all relevant test results relating to) the New Release (or, as the case may be, replace the Maintained Software or any part of it with the New Release) and shall, if requested by the Customer, assist the Customer with testing the same.  7.4  If any New Release that is required to Fix a Defect fails to meet the Acceptance Criteria (a 'Failure'), the Customer shall notify the Supplier in writing, providing reasons for the Failure[,the Supplier shall rectify the Failure as soon as reasonably practicable and the testing process set out in clause 7.3 shall be repeated until the New Release meets the Acceptance Criteria or the Parties otherwise agree to dispense with further acceptance testing].  7.5  If any New Release fails to meet the Acceptance Criteria the Supplier shall cooperate with the Customer in decommissioning the New Release and returning the Maintained Software to its state before the acceptance tests.  7.6  Any New Release accepted by the Customer in accordance with clause 7.4 shall be deemed to form part of the Maintained Software.  7.7  The Supplier shall provide the Customer with any New Release accepted by the Customer at no additional charge, and the Charges will not be increased as a result of the Customer's Use of the New Release, unless otherwise agreed in the Order Form.  7.8  If requested by the Customer, the Supplier shall provide training to designated Customer Personnel to enable them to make full and proper Use of any New Release as soon as reasonably practicable after the delivery, installation or acceptance of the New Release, at the times and locations specified by the Customer (acting reasonably). Such training shall be at no additional cost to the Customer.  7.9  The Customer shall not be obliged to accept or Use any New Release, and any decision by the Customer not to accept or Use any New Release shall not have any adverse effect on the Supplier's obligations to provide the Software Maintenance Services, [(save that the Supplier shall not be liable to the Customer for any failure to Fix a Defect in accordance with clause 4 to the extent that such failure is caused by the Customer's failure to Use a New Release which is required to Fix the Defect where such New Release has met the Acceptance Criteria].   7.10  The Supplier shall provide the Software Maintenance Services for:   7.10.1  the current version of the Maintained Software and, as a minimum, the two previous versions of the Maintained Software; and  7.10.2  each version of the Software for a minimum period of two years,  save to the extent otherwise provided in the Order Form.   7.11  The Supplier shall provide Release Management services to ensure that versions of Maintained Software are audited and logged, and that changes to such Maintained Software are deployed into the Services Environment in a controlled manner. The Supplier shall provide the Customer with a detailed Release Plan to be accompanied for each release.  7.12  With respect to the Maintained Software, the Supplier will:  7.12.1  notify the Customer of, and explain and demonstrate the effect to the Customer of, each major release and minor release;  7.12.2  provide any training for Customer Personnel necessitated by the provision of major releases and minor releases for such Maintained Software; and  7.12.3  notify the Customer of and provide a risk assessment of any patches and critical vulnerability exploits released by the relevant vendor in relation to the Maintained Software.  7.13  Unless otherwise requested by the Customer, all Changes to the Maintained Software (including major releases, minor releases, patch releases, new versions etc) or deployment of new Maintained Software, must be:  7.13.1  approved by the Customer in writing in accordance with the Change & Release Management Process; and   7.13.2  planned, scheduled and deployed or installed in a manner that has the minimum business impact or disruption to the Customer Group.

clauses.clause_key: Service Levels/Key Performance Indicators (KPI)@ Cloud Services Schedule
clauses.description: Clause 8 (Availability)   Cloud Services Schedule     8.  AVAILABILITY  8.1  The Supplier shall provide to the Customer all Service Levels applicable to the Services, which shall be included in the Order Form. Subject to clause 8.2, the Supplier shall ensure that: (a) the Availability of the Cloud Services meets or exceeds the Service Level Standards identified in the Service Levels; and (b) the Support and Maintenance Services meet or exceed the Support and Maintenance Service Levels.  8.2  Any lack of Availability of the Cloud Services that arises as a result of:  8.2.1  the Customer's or any of its Authorised Users' misuse of the Cloud Services;  8.2.2  the failure of the Customer's or any other Authorised Users' internet connectivity;  8.2.3  internet or other network traffic problems other than problems arising in or from networks provided (or required to be provided) or controlled by the Supplier;   8.2.4  any failure of the Customer Group Systems to comply with the requirements of the Specifications or Documentation; or  8.2.5  Scheduled Downtime,  shall not be taken into account for the purposes of calculating whether the Supplier has met the Availability Requirement.  8.3  The Supplier shall notify the Customer at least seventy two hours (72) in advance of all Scheduled Downtime. All such scheduled outages shall: (i) last no longer than one (1) hour each; (ii) be scheduled during the Maintenance Window; and (iii) occur no more frequently than the Scheduled Outage Limit. The Supplier shall use all reasonable endeavours to ensure that Scheduled Downtime does not take place during regular business hours of the relevant Customer Site.   8.4  In the event: (a) the Availability falls below the levels specified in the Service Levels in (i) two (2) consecutive calendar months or (ii) any three (3), or more, non consecutive months in any twelve (12) month period; or (b) the Availability falls below 95% in any given month, then, notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the Contract Document, the Customer shall have the option (but not the obligation) to terminate the affected Order Form or the entire Contract Document with immediate effect, with written notice to Supplier and without the requirement to pay any early termination Charges.    Clauses 10 (Service Levels, Service Level Reporting and Service Level Credits)   Cloud Services Schedule     10.1  The Supplier shall provide monthly service level reports to the Customer ('Service Level Reports'). The Service Level Reports shall be provided in a mutually agreed format and the Supplier shall contain at least the following information:   10.1.1  without prejudice to clause 10.8, the actual performance of the Services against the Service Levels in the past month;  10.1.2  where applicable, the reason or cause for the Services not meeting the Service Levels;   10.1.3  the specific remedial action the Supplier has undertaken or will undertake to minimise impact of such Service Level failure on the Customer and to the ensure the Service Levels will be subsequently achieved; and  10.1.4  any Service Credits due to the Customer.   10.2  Where in accordance with the Service Level Reports the Services have failed to meet the applicable Service Levels in the past month the Supplier shall immediately pay the applicable Service Credits identified in the Service Level Reports, to the Customer and indicate such payment in the subsequent invoices in accordance with clause 10.3 below.   10.3  The Service Credits may be recovered by the Customer, in its absolute discretion, either as a sum of money payable by the Supplier or as a credit against any sum of money which may subsequently be due to the Supplier under the Order Form or otherwise. The Supplier acknowledges and agrees that the Service Credits are not the sole and exclusive remedy and are in addition to, and do not limit, any other rights or remedies available to the Customer, under the Contract Document, Applicable Laws or otherwise, in respect of the Supplier's failure to meet a Service Level.   10.4  The Supplier shall not be excused or absolved from its obligation to adhere to the Service Levels and Availability Requirements during a Force Majeure Event. For the purposes of this Cloud Services Schedule, Force Majeure Event shall not include (i) disruptions to the Supplier's telecommunication or network services or power supply; (ii) any delays or failures that arise due to the Supplier's failure to exercise its due diligence; or (iii) any delays or failures due to factors that are within the reasonable control of the Supplier (including difficulties with Supplier Personnel or other labour difficulties, and business continuity and disaster recovery procedures).   10.5  The Parties acknowledge and agree that:   10.5.1  the Service Credits are a price adjustment to reflect the reduced level of Services performed by the Supplier; and  10.5.2  loss or damage that may be suffered by the Customer or any other Customer Group member as a result of the Supplier's failure to ensure that the Services meet the Service Levels exceeds the Service Credits, and the Service Credits are not penalties or liquidated damages.  10.6  The Supplier shall implement and maintain appropriate measuring and monitoring tools and procedures at least in accordance with Good Industry Practice that at minimum:  10.6.1  are capable of measuring the Supplier's performance against the Service Levels;   10.6.2  permit reporting (and the Supplier shall report to the Customer) at a level of detail sufficient to determine compliance with the Service Levels, including metrics to evidence compliance with Service Levels; and  10.6.3  are approved by the Customer (such approval not to be unreasonably withheld).  10.7  Without prejudice to the foregoing, the Supplier shall notify the Customer immediately and in writing of:  10.7.1  the occurrence of (and, to the extent Supplier is reasonably able, prior to the occurrence of) each failure to meet a Service Level;  10.7.2  any delays, problems or degradations (or anticipated delays, problems or degradations) from time to time in the performance of the Cloud Services; and  10.7.3  any circumstances from time to time (actual or potential) which may prevent or hinder the Supplier from providing the Cloud Services,   and shall, immediately, provide the Customer with:  10.7.4  in the case of any actual failure to meet a Service Level, delay, problem or degradation, a detailed analysis of its root cause and recommendations as to how it can be remedied and not repeated; and  10.7.5  in the case of any anticipated failure to meet a Service Level, delays, problems or degradations, recommendations as to how it can be avoided and, if this is not possible, remedied and not repeated.  10.8  In the event the Cloud Services do not meet the Availability Requirements for (a) three (3) months consecutively; or (b) any three (3) months during a consecutive six (6) month period, then, in addition to all other remedies available to the Customer under the Contract Document or Applicable Laws, the Customer shall be entitled to terminate the Contract Document upon written notice to the Supplier with no further liability, expense, or obligation to the Supplier.    Clause 12.1.1 (Performance and Monitoring)   Cloud Services Schedule     12.1  The Supplier shall:   12.1.1  implement and maintain Customer approved measuring and monitoring tools to monitor the Supplier's performance of its obligations under the Contract Document and to provide reports to the Customer in accordance with the Order Form;   12.1.2  immediately disclose to the Customer in writing any change in circumstances that may have a material impact on the Supplier's ability to perform its obligations in accordance with:   12.1.2(A)  the Contract Document; or  12.1.2(B)  Applicable Laws;  12.1.3  notify the Customer as soon as practicable of any changes to be made to or any adverse developments with respect to the Infrastructure that may have an impact on the provision of the Cloud Services including its inability to comply with the Contract Document or Applicable Laws; and   12.1.4  provide and discuss recommendations with the Customer to avoid any anticipated impact on the Cloud Services arising from sub clause 12.1.2 or 12.1.3 above, or to avoid, mitigate or rectify any Cloud Service issue disclosed from the reports, review meetings or audits under the Contract Document, and the Supplier shall take such action (including the use of additional resources) as is necessary to avoid, remedy and otherwise minimise the Cloud Service impact or issue in accordance with Good Industry Practice and the Service Levels.   12.2  The Customer may terminate the Contract Document with immediate effect if there is any change in circumstances that may have a material impact on the Supplier's ability to carry out the Services effectively and in compliance with the Contract Document or Applicable Laws.

clauses.clause_key: Service Levels/Key Performance Indicators (KPI)@ IT Outsourced Managed Services Schedule
clauses.description: Clause 28 (Service Review)  28.1  The Supplier shall schedule and conduct monthly (or more frequently as requested by the Customer in writing) performance review meetings with the Customer, providing the Customer with aligned reporting, trend analysis and actively identifying service improvement opportunities, including:  28.1.1  reviewing the attainment of Service Levels [and Key Performance Indicators];   28.1.5 28.1.5  notifying the Customer of planned and emergency outages and changes, and communicating the event as required for the Customer to understand which components of the Services Environment that will be affected, the impact and times of unavailability of the Services Environment.     Clause 32 (Service Levels and Service Credits)  32.1  The Supplier shall ensure that the Managed Services are delivered and provided in a manner that meets or exceeds the applicable Service Levels.  32.2  If a Service Level is not specified for a particular Managed Service, the Supplier shall ensure that the Managed Service is provided in a manner that meets or exceeds Good Industry Practice.  32.3  The achievement of the Service Levels by the Supplier may require the coordinated, collaborative effort of the Supplier with its Subcontractors, other Supplier Affiliates and Customer Group Service Providers. The Supplier shall provide a single point of contact for the prompt resolution of all failures to achieve Service Levels, regardless of whether the reason for such failure was caused by the Supplier.  32.4  If the Managed Services fail to meet the Service Levels, the Supplier shall:  32.4.1  pay the applicable Service Credits to the Customer; and  32.4.2  do all things necessary to:   32.4.2(A)  remedy the failure as soon as reasonably practicable;   32.4.2(B)  minimise the impact of such failure; and   32.4.2(C)  prevent the failure from occurring again, including by providing all necessary and additional resources and re performing the affected Managed Services.  32.5  The Supplier acknowledges and agrees that the Service Credits are not a sole and exclusive remedy and are in addition to, and do not limit, any other rights or remedies available to the Customer, under the Contract Document, statute, common law or otherwise, in respect of the Supplier's failure to meet a Service Level.   32.6  The Service Credits may be recovered by the Customer, in its absolute discretion, either as a sum of money payable by the Supplier or as a credit against any sum of money which may subsequently be due to the Supplier under the Order Form or otherwise.  32.7  The Parties acknowledge and agree that:   32.7.1  the Service Credits are a price adjustment to reflect the reduced level of Managed Services performed by the Supplier; and   32.7.2  loss or damage that may be suffered by the Customer or any other Customer Group member as a result of the Supplier's failure to ensure that the Managed Services meet the Service Levels exceeds the Service Credits, and the Service Credits are not penalties or liquidated damages.   32.8  The Supplier shall implement and maintain measuring and monitoring tools and procedures that:  32.8.1  are capable of measuring the Supplier's performance against the Service Levels;   32.8.2  permit reporting (and the Supplier shall report to the Customer) at a level of detail sufficient to determine compliance with the Service Levels; and  32.8.3  are approved by the Customer (such approval not to be unreasonably withheld).  32.9  Without prejudice to the foregoing, the Supplier shall notify the Customer promptly and in writing of:  32.9.1  the occurrence of (and, to the extent Supplier is reasonably able, prior to the occurrence of) each failure to meet a Service Level;  32.9.2  any delays, problems or degradations (or anticipated delays, problems or degradations) from time to time in the performance of the Managed Services; and  32.9.3  any circumstances from time to time (actual or potential) which may prevent or hinder the Supplier from performing the Managed Services,   and shall, as soon as reasonably practicable, provide the Customer with:  32.9.4  in the case of any actual failure to meet a Service Level, delay, problem or degradation, a detailed analysis of its root cause and recommendations as to how it can be remedied and not repeated; and  32.9.5  in the case of any anticipated failure to meet a Service Level, delays, problems or degradations, recommendations as to how it can be avoided and, if this is not possible, remedied and not repeated.  32.10  The Parties shall discuss these recommendations and the Supplier shall then take such action (including the use of additional resources) as is necessary to avoid, remedy and otherwise minimise the impact of the actual or anticipated delays, problems, degradations or circumstances (as the case may be) in accordance with Good Industry Practice.       Clause 37.1.1 (Performance and Monitoring)     37.1  The Supplier shall:   37.1.1  implement and maintain measuring and monitoring tools to monitor the Supplier's performance of its obligations under the Contract Document and to provide reports to the Customer in accordance with the Order Form;

  6.7.2  permit reporting (and the Supplier shall report to the Customer) at a level of detail sufficient to determine compliance with the Service Levels, including metrics to evidence compliance with Service Levels; and  6.7.3  are approved by the Customer (such approval not to be unreasonably withheld).  6.8  Without prejudice to the foregoing, the Supplier shall notify the Customer immediately and in writing of:  6.8.1  the occurrence of (and, to the extent Supplier is reasonably able, prior to the occurrence of) each failure to meet a Service Level;  6.8.2  any delays, problems or degradations (or anticipated delays, problems or degradations) from time to time in the performance of the Services; and  6.8.3  any circumstances from time to time (actual or potential) which may prevent or hinder the Supplier from performing the Services,   and shall, immediately, provide the Customer with:  6.8.4  in the case of any actual failure to meet a Service Level, delay, problem or degradation, a detailed analysis of its root cause and recommendations as to how it can be remedied and not repeated; and  6.8.5  in the case of any anticipated failure to meet a Service Level, delays, problems or degradations, recommendations as to how it can be avoided and, if this is not possible, remedied and not repeated.  6.9  The Supplier shall then take such action (including the use of additional resources) as is necessary to avoid, remedy and otherwise minimise the impact of the actual or anticipated delays, problems, degradations or circumstances (as the case may be) in a manner acceptable to the Customer.  6.10  In the event the Data Centre Services do not meet the Availability Requirements for (a) three (3) months consecutively; or (b) any three (3) months during a consecutive six (6) month period, then, in addition to all other remedies available to the Customer under the Contract Document or Applicable Laws, the Customer shall be entitled to terminate the Contract Document upon written notice to the Supplier with no further liability, expense, or obligation to the Supplier. clauses.description: Clause 6 (Availability, Service Levels, and Service Credits)     6.1  The Supplier shall provide all Network Services in accordance with the Availability and Service Levels applicable to the Network Services which shall be set out in the Order Form.   6.2  In the event that the Supplier is in breach of any Service Levels, the Customer shall be entitled to claim Service Credits in the manner agreed in the Order Form. clauses.clause_key: Service Levels/Key Performance Indicators (KPI)@ Business Process Outsourcing Schedule
clauses.description: Clause 9   PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT  Service Levels  9.1  Throughout the Order Form Term:  (a)  the Supplier shall ensure that the Services meet or exceed the Service Levels.  (b)  the Services shall be provided at all times during the Service Hours, unless otherwise stated in the Order Form.  Service Credits  9.2  If the Services fail to meet the Service Levels, the Supplier shall:  (a)  pay to the Customer the applicable Service Credits; and  (b)  do all things necessary to remedy the failure as soon as reasonably practicable, to minimise the impact of such failure and prevent the failure from occurring again, including providing all necessary and additional resources and re performing the Services.  9.3  Service Credits are not a sole and exclusive remedy, and are in addition to, and do not limit, any other rights or remedies available to the Customer, under the Contract Document, statute, common law or otherwise, in respect of the Supplier's failure to meet the Service Levels.  9.4  The Service Credits are a debt due by the Supplier and the Supplier shall either deduct Service Credits due against the next invoice (if there is no invoice against which to deduct them, the Supplier shall promptly pay them to the Customer after the end of the period to which they relate).  9.5  Service Credits shall:  (a)  be recoverable despite any termination of the Order Form for any reason (including where the applicable Service Level(s) still has not been met by the time of termination); and  (b)  continue to accrue up to the completion of exit management, despite termination of the Contract Document.  9.6  The Parties acknowledge and agree that Service Credits:  (a)  represent a refund for Charges for the value of Services not supplied, not supplied in time, or not supplied in accordance with the requirements stated in the Order Form;  (b)  reflect the Customer's legitimate interest in avoiding a Service Failure because of the adverse impact it would have on its business operations and its customers;  (c)  have been negotiated by Parties of similar bargaining strength who have had the benefit of legal advice; and  (d)  shall not preclude the Customer from recovering damages for the actual Losses suffered by the Customer (provided that such damages shall be reduced by an amount equal to the Service Credits paid by the Supplier in respect of the same Losses).  Service Failures  9.7  The Supplier shall procure that all Service Failures and other degradations in the Services are kept to an absolute minimum.  9.8  Without limiting any other right or remedy of the Customer under the Contract Document or under Applicable Law and notwithstanding any other provision in the Contract Document, the Supplier shall take all such additional steps and apply all such additional resources in order to remedy any Service Failure as soon as reasonably practicable to minimise the impact of such Service Failure. Without prejudice to the foregoing, the Supplier shall notify the Customer in writing:  (a)  immediately following the occurrence of (and, to the extent the Supplier is reasonably able, prior to the occurrence of) each Service Failure;  (b)  promptly in the event of any delays, problems or degradations (or anticipated delays, problems or degradations) from time to time in the supply of the Services (or any of them); and  (c)  promptly in the event of any circumstances from time to time (actual or potential) which may prevent or hinder the Supplier from supplying the Services,  and shall, as soon as reasonably practicable and at its own cost and expense, provide the Customer with:  (d)  in the case of any actual Service Failures, delays, problems or degradations, a detailed analysis of the root cause and recommendations as to how such Service Failures, delays, problems or degradations in Services can be remedied and not repeated;  (e)  the Supplier's procedures for correcting the Service Failures and ensuring that there will not be a recurrence of the Service Failures, delays, problems or degradations in the Services; and  (f)  in the case of any anticipated Service Failures, delays, problems or degradations, recommendations as to how they can be avoided and, if this is not possible, remedied and not repeated.  9.9  The Parties shall discuss these recommendations and the Supplier shall then take such action (including the use of additional resources) as is necessary to avoid, remedy and otherwise minimise the impact of the actual or anticipated Service Failures, delays, problems, degradations or circumstances (as the case may be) in accordance with Good Industry Practice.  Performance reporting  9.10  The Supplier shall implement and maintain measuring and monitoring tools and procedures that:  (a)  are capable of measuring the Supplier's performance against the Service Levels;  (b)  permit reporting (and the Supplier shall report to the Customer) at a level of detail sufficient to determine compliance with the Service Levels; and  (c)  are approved by the Customer (such approval not to be unreasonably withheld).  9.11  The Parties shall discuss these recommendations and the Supplier shall then take such action (including the use of additional resources) as is necessary to avoid, remedy and otherwise minimise the impact of the actual or anticipated delays, problems, degradations or circumstances (as the case may be) in accordance with Good Industry Practice.

clauses.description: Clause 30   General terms and Conditions     30.1  The Supplier shall not subcontract any of its obligations under the Contract Document without the Customer's prior written consent.  30.2  Prior to or with no less than 90 days written notice ahead of subcontracting any of its obligations under the Contract Document, the Supplier shall notify the Customer of its intention to subcontract its obligations and shall provide the Customer with details of any potential subcontractors, including any change to Subcontractors.  30.3  Where the Supplier engages a Subcontractor under the Contract Document, the Supplier shall:  30.3.1  provide the Customer with such details on the Subcontractors reasonably required by the Customer from time to time, including the screening conducted by the Supplier in respect of such Subcontractors, the location of the Subcontractors and the controls put in place by the Supplier to ensure that such Subcontractors perform their relevant tasks in accordance with the Contract Document;   30.3.2  ensure that Subcontractors comply with all of the applicable terms and conditions of the Contract Document applying to the Supplier and references to Supplier in the Contract Document shall instead be to that Subcontractor;  30.3.3  remain liable to the Customer for the performance of the subcontracted obligations performed by Subcontractors under the Contract Document as if acts or omissions of a Subcontractor were acts or omissions of the Supplier;  30.3.4  ensure that all subcontracts with Subcontractors are made in writing and are consistent with the terms and conditions of the Contract Document;   30.3.5  ensure that subcontracts with Subcontractors are capable of being novated, transferred or assigned to the Customer without any additional costs to the Customer; and  30.3.6  promptly notify the Customer in the event of any breach by the Subcontractor of the terms and conditions of the relevant subcontract.   30.4  The Supplier shall notify the Customer of any changes to Subcontractors in accordance with clause 30.2 and shall seek the Customer's consent to such changes in Subcontractors prior to such change. The Customer shall have the right to object to such changes in Subcontractors.   30.5  If the Supplier engages a Subcontractor or makes any changes to Subcontractors without having obtained the Customer's consent in accordance with this clause 30, this shall constitute a material breach of the Contract Document and, without prejudice to any other remedy the Customer may have under the Contract Document or at law, the Customer shall be entitled to terminate the Contract Document in accordance with clause 20.3.1.   30.6  If:   30.6.1  a Subcontractor commits a material breach of any relevant subcontract;   30.6.2  the Customer reasonably believes that a Subcontractor's performance of its obligations is unsatisfactory (and has not been rectified within a reasonable time period);   30.6.3  the Customer reasonably believes that the continuing engagement of a Subcontractor is materially damaging, or is likely to materially damage, the reputation or brand of any Customer Group member; or  30.6.4  the Supplier engages a Subcontractor in breach of this clause 30,  then the Customer may, by notice, require the Supplier to promptly terminate the subcontract with the Subcontractor in question, and require the Supplier to have those Supplies performed by an alternative Subcontractor or by the Supplier itself, as soon as reasonably practicable and the Supplier shall comply with any such requirement from the Customer.       Clause 3   Subcontracting   Outsourcing Annexure    3.1  In addition to the requirements set out in the clause [30] (Subcontracting) of the General Terms and Conditions, the following provisions shall apply to any subcontracting arrangements.   3.2  The Supplier shall obtain prior specific or general written authorisation from the Customer before transferring data to its Subcontractors.  3.3  If:   3.3.1  the Customer has concerns about the financial viability of the Subcontractor, which Supplier is unable to resolve to the Customer's reasonable satisfaction;  3.3.2  the Customer can demonstrate that the actions or omissions of a Subcontractor have caused damage to the reputation or brand of Customer Group, or have resulted in adverse publicity for the Customer Group;  3.3.3  in the Customer's reasonable opinion, there are Applicable Laws which require that any of the obligations of the Supplier which have been subcontracted should cease to be subcontracted;  3.3.4  a Regulatory Authority directs, recommends or issues a guideline in respect of the Customer's regulatory obligations that the Subcontractor should not continue to be used to perform the Supplier's subcontracted obligations, and the Supplier is not able, within a reasonable period of time, to resolve the Regulatory Authority's concerns;  3.3.5  the subcontracting arrangement could have a material adverse effect on the Customer's material function (including if a Subcontractor refuses to grant audit rights and/or to comply with all laws and contractual obligations); or  3.3.6  the subcontracting arrangement materially increases the risk for the Customer,  then the Parties agree the following process shall apply:  3.3.7  the Parties shall first meet to discuss the proposed revocation or direction which shall include a discussion on:  (A)  any potential adverse impact on the Supplies or the Service Levels;  (B)  the changes (if any) required to the Supplies;  (C)  the impact (if any) to the Charges; and  (D)  the plan and timeline for the Supplier to remove and replace the Subcontractor including transitioning the services from the existing Subcontractor to the Supplier or a new Subcontractor; and  3.3.8  if after having met to discuss the matters referred to in sub clause above, the Customer elects to exercise its right to revoke approval of a Subcontractor or direct the removal and replacement of a Subcontractor, then:  (A)  the Supplier shall promptly prepare a plan detailing those matters discussed and agreed by the Parties under paragraph (a) above ('Subcontractor Transition Plan'), for the Customer's review, comment and approval; and  (B)  following approval by the Customer, the Supplier shall implement the Subcontractor Transition Plan and remove and replace the relevant Subcontractor in accordance with that Subcontractor Transition Plan.  3.3.9  If the Parties cannot agree to a Subcontractor Transition Plan or the Supplier fails to comply with a Subcontractor Transition Plan agreed between the Parties, then the Customer may, by notice, require the Supplier to promptly terminate the subcontract with the Subcontractor in question as soon as reasonably practicable, and require the Supplier to have the Supplies performed by an alternative Subcontractor or by the Supplier itself, and the Supplier shall comply with any such requirement from the Customer. For the avoidance of doubt, the Supplier's failure to terminate the subcontract with the relevant Subcontractor shall constitute a material breach of the Contract Document.  3.4  A Subcontractor will not be entitled to further subcontract the performance of the Services without the Customer's prior approval. Clause [30] (Subcontracting) of the General Terms and Conditions shall apply to the approval and conduct of any subcontractors engaged by the Subcontractor.     3.5  [Without limiting the Customer's other rights in the Contract Document, if the Customer terminates a Contract Document in accordance with the Clause [20.3] (Termination for Cause) of the General Terms and Conditions, or if a Regulatory Authority instructs, directs or gives guidance for the Customer to terminate the Contract Document, the Customer may at its option, upon written notice to the Supplier, require the Supplier to novate any or all of the Supplier's Key Subcontracts, to the Customer [or the Customer's nominee], with immediate effect. The Supplier shall do all things necessary to effect such novation, including executing a novation agreement and ensuring that all such Key Subcontractors execute the same.  3.6  The Supplier will not permit Key Subcontracts to terminate or expire in respect of the subcontracted Services without the Customer's prior approval (not to be unreasonably withheld).]

clauses.description: Clause 4   APPROVED LOCATIONS   Outsourcing Annexure    4.1  The Supplier shall only perform its obligations from:   4.1.1  the Customer Sites;   4.1.2  the Supplier locations agreed in an Order Form; and  4.1.3  any dedicated facilities specified in an Order Form,  ('Service Locations').   4.2  The Supplier shall not perform any of its obligations from a location other than the Service Locations unless the Parties agree in writing to a Change.  4.3  Before any Change to the Service Locations is made, the Supplier shall:   4.3.1  develop a migration plan to avoid any disruptions or degradation in the Services during the migration period, for the Customer's written approval, and comply with that plan once it is approved. For the purposes of this paragraph 4.3.1, the Customer shall within ten (10) Business Days after receiving the Supplier's draft migration plan, provide to the Supplier a written response indicating whether it accepts, rejects or proposes a modification or variation to any aspect of the migration plan. If the Customer requests a modification or variation to the draft migration plan, then the Parties shall promptly meet and act in good faith to agree in writing on variations to the draft migration plan which are acceptable to the Customer. If the Customer rejects the draft migration plan, the Supplier shall submit to the Customer a revised draft migration plan no later than five (5) Business Days after receipt of the notice from the Customer that the draft migration plan is rejected, and the process in this paragraph 4.3.1 shall start again until the Customer approves the migration plan. If the Customer does not approve the migration plan in accordance with this paragraph 4.3.1, then the Service Locations shall remain unchanged;  4.3.2  comply with this paragraph 4.3 and the obligations relating to data protection and information security set out in the Data Privacy Annexure and the Information Security Annexure before any Customer Confidential Information is transferred to the new Service Locations; and  4.3.3  demonstrate to the Customer's satisfaction that:  (A)  the relevant location meets all applicable requirements set out in the Contract Document;  (B)  the change in location will not impact the Supplier's ability to meet its obligations under the Contract Document;   (C)  any particular security, confidentiality or privacy concerns that the Customer may have with the proposed variation have been addressed to the Customer's satisfaction; and  (D)  the Customer will continue to be able to receive the full benefit of the Contract Document, and to be able fully to exercise all of its rights under the Contract Document.  4.4  A change in Service Locations:   4.4.1  shall not result in any increase in the Charges payable by the Customer; and  4.4.2  the project costs associated with migrating the Services from the existing Service Location to a new Service Location shall be borne by the Supplier unless the Customer has specifically requested a change in Service Location.   4.5  In respect of any dedicated facility specified in an Order Form, the Supplier shall ensure:   4.5.1  the Customer, the Customer's Representatives and any Regulatory Authority is provided with unfettered access to such dedicated facility to inspect and observe the performance of the Services by the Supplier; and  4.5.2  access to such dedicated facility is restricted to:   (A)  the Customer and the Customer's Representatives;   (B)  the Supplier Personnel authorised by the Customer in writing to access such dedicated facility; and  (C)  any Regulatory Authorities,   and no other person shall access such dedicated facilities.  4.6  In the event that a change in location of a dedicated facility results in any cost savings to the Supplier in the provision of the Services, the Supplier shall pass on a proportion of such cost savings to the Customer, as agreed by the Parties (acting reasonably), as a reduction in the Charges payable under the relevant Order Form.   4.7  The Supplier shall not use any dedicated facility as a reference site for any other customer or potential customer of the Supplier unless the Supplier obtains the Customer's prior written consent to such use.      Clause 12.11.2   2.11  The Supplier shall notify the Customer as soon as practicable from when it first becomes aware of:  2.11.2  any change to the country location(s) or data centres where the Customer Confidential Information is retained, hosted or accessed from.
