clauses.description: Clause 15.4.1(Confidential Information)   General Terms and Conditions      15.4  The following circumstances shall not constitute a breach of the obligations of confidence set out in this clause 15 by the Recipient Party where Confidential Information is disclosed:  15.4.1  to the Recipient Party's Personnel and professional advisers to the extent necessary to enable the Recipient Party to exercise its rights and perform its obligations under the Contract Document, provided that prior to disclosure such Personnel and professional advisers have entered into an appropriate written confidentiality undertaking or non disclosure agreement with the Recipient Party to treat and keep such Confidential Information confidential on terms no less onerous than contained in the Contract Document    Clause 2.3. of the Information Security Annexure     2.3  Upon the Customer's written request, the Supplier shall within such reasonable notice as may be stipulated by the Customer and during working hours, complete the Bank's Third Party Security Assessment (which may be conducted onsite or remotely) providing full and accurate responses, and provide any relevant information, documentation, reports and Technology Records requested by the Bank to verify the Supplier's compliance with the Information Security Controls, and any certifications (if available) as specified in paragraph 2.4.   At the Customer's election, such TPSA may be conducted onsite or remotely by the Customer or its authorised representatives, a Regulatory Authority or an independent inspection body with the required professional qualifications selected by the Customer, subject to their respective duty of confidentiality to the Customer. The TPSA shall not exceed a frequency of one per calendar year, unless as directed by a Regulatory Authority, a Material Cyber Security Incident had occurred or there is a material change to the service scope or a change in the events described in paragraph 2.11, in that case additional incremental security assessment may occur.    Clause 24.1.6 (General Indemnity)   General Terms and Conditions    24.1  The Supplier shall at all times, whether during or after the Contract Document Term, defend, indemnify and keep indemnified the Indemnified Parties from and against Losses brought against or incurred by any of the Indemnified Parties (including any Losses which any of the Indemnified Parties agrees to pay by way of settlement or compromise) to the extent related to or arising, directly or indirectly, out of:  24.1.6  breach of clause 15 (Confidential Information), or any provision of the Regulatory Schedule by the Supplier or Supplier Personnel. clauses.description: Clause 2.1.1   Information Security Annexure    2.1  The Supplier shall comply with the following security measures in relation to Customer Confidential Information (including Personal Data) ('Information Security Controls') (and in the event of any conflict between any of the Information Security Controls below, the most stringent or higher level of security standard shall apply):  2.1.1  take all appropriate physical, technical and organisational security measures and observe Good Industry Practice to protect Customer Confidential Information, in particular against accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Customer Confidential Information transmitted, stored or otherwise Processed. This shall include measures to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of the Supplier Systems and services (as applicable), and the ability to restore the availability and access to the Customer Confidential Information in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical security incident.     Clause 3.1(c) (Processing and Segregation of Personal Data)    Data Privacy Annexure (Controller to Processor)    3.1  The Supplier shall and shall ensure that Supplier Personnel and its Sub processors shall in respect of Personal Data Processed pursuant to the Contract Document:  (c)  implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in accordance with applicable Data Protection Laws, Good Industry Practice, the Information Security Controls and shall in any event comprise measures that are no less stringent than those used to process the Supplier's own confidential information and Personal Data.     Clause 1.11   Data Privacy Annexure (Controller to Controller)    1.11  Without prejudice to the Supplier's obligations to comply with the Information Security Controls under paragraph 6 of the of the Regulatory Schedule of the Agreement, each party shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk presented by the Processing of the Shared Personal Data including having regard to the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of Data Subjects, in particular from any event or security incident that may lead to or leads to a Data Breach.     Clause 1.3 (e)   Data Privacy Annexure (Joint Controller)    1.3  The Parties agree that they will each:  (e)  implement, and assist the other Party to implement, appropriate technical and organisational security measures at a minimum to the level set out in the Information Security Controls to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk presented by Processing the Personal Data, in particular from a Data Breach. For the avoidance of doubt, the Supplier shall comply with the Information Security Controls (as defined under the Agreement) at all times.     Clause 3.1(k) Processing and Segregation of Personal Data)   Data Privacy Annexure (Controller to Processor)    3.1  The Supplier shall and shall ensure that Supplier Personnel and its Sub processors shall in respect of Personal Data Processed pursuant to the Contract Document:  (k)  segregate Personal Data held and Processed pursuant to the Contract Document from its own data and from any data of its other customers    Clause 5.7 (Data Leakage prevention)   ICS Schedule     If Supplier Staff has been provided with a Corporate Owned Device (COD) or are permitted to use a personal mobile device under the Bring Your Own Mobile Device (BYOMD) scheme to Process SC Group Data, the Supplier must deploy appropriate security measures to protect SC Group Data on such devices, including:  a)  deploy appropriate mobile device management (MDM) applications;   b)  containerisation of SC Group Data (i.e. SC Group Data is segregated and secured from other data on the COD or the BYOMD);  c)  implement and configure remote wipe capabilities; and  d)  enforce automatic updates and periodic scans of the devices, to check for jailbreaking/rooting/tampering and malware.

clauses.clause_key: Record Retention, Information and Audit Rights
clauses.description: Clause 9 (Audit)   Regulatory Schedule     9.  AUDIT  Customer and Customer Group audits and inspections  9.1  Subject to Applicable Law, the Supplier shall maintain a complete and accurate audit trail and maintain records and supporting documentation of all financial and non financial transactions relating to the Contract Document for a period of no less than seven (7) years after termination or expiry of the Contract Document (or, as applicable, the Order Form). The Supplier shall assist the Customer and Customer Group to maintain such records and supporting documentation at the Customer Group's premises and shall make such information available to the Customer and Customer Group on a timely basis.  9.2  The Supplier shall ensure that the Customer, Customer Group, their Auditors and/or Regulatory Authorities may perform audits and inspections of the Supplier and any Subcontractors to:  9.2.1  verify the make up and accuracy of all Charges and Invoices;  9.2.2  verify the integrity of the Customer and/or Customer Group Confidential Information and examine the systems that process, store, support and transmit the Customer and/or Customer Group Confidential Information;  9.2.3  examine the Supplier's performance of its obligations under the Contract Document; and  9.2.4  ascertain compliance with the audit, reporting, disclosure, accounting or regulatory requirements of the Customer or any Customer Group member.    9.3  Without limiting paragraph 9.2, the Customer, Customer Group, their Auditors and/or Regulatory Authorities have the right to audit and inspect:  9.3.1  the resources, systems and infrastructure used by the Supplier and any Subcontractor in connection with the performance of the Supplier's obligations under the Contract Document;  9.3.2  practices, policies and procedures of the Supplier and any Subcontractor (including security procedures, disaster recovery and business continuity procedures), relating to the Contract Document;  9.3.3  any premises from which the Services are being performed or provided, including any premises at which Customer and/or Customer Group Confidential Information is processed;   9.3.4  financial and operational records of the Supplier and Subcontractors, excluding any information relating to the costs and margin of the Supplier or any Subcontractor;  9.3.5  the security and integrity of Customer Confidential Information and examine the Supplier Support Systems that Process, store, support and transmit Customer Confidential Information in connection with the Contract Document;   9.3.6  the Approved Locations, and any other premises from which the Deliverables and Services are being performed or provided, including any premises at which Customer Confidential Information is Processed;   9.3.7  take copies of relevant documentation on site if they are critical to the operations of the ICT third party service provider; and  9.3.8  run their own software on the Supplier Systems for the purposes of carrying out an audit or inspection.  9.4  Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, as part of an audit or inspection, the Customer or any Auditor may conduct:  9.4.1  penetration testing on the Services and any Supplier Systems; and  9.4.2  threat and vulnerability risk assessments in respect of the Services and Supplier Systems.  9.5  The Customer and the Auditor's rights of audit and inspection shall extend to the Supplier's Subcontractors.  9.6  The Supplier shall and shall ensure that the Subcontractors regularly conduct internal audits to ensure the adequacy of and compliance with the Supplier Group's risk and contingency policies and procedures.    9.7  The Supplier shall ensure that all Supplier Personnel are appropriately trained in the effective use of the risk management and contingency policies and procedures of the Supplier Group.  9.8  The Supplier shall, and shall ensure that its Subcontractors, provide all reasonable assistance and cooperation to the Customer, Customer Group, their Auditors and/or Regulatory Authorities including:  9.8.1  promptly making available to the Customer, Customer Group, Auditors and/or Regulatory Authorities (and in any event, no later than seven (7) days following a request from the Customer, Customer  Group,  Auditors and/or Regulatory Authorities to do so, or such lesser time period if required to comply with Applicable Law or direction from a Regulatory Authority or as may be necessary if there is an emergency or crisis situation or where such period of notice would result in an ineffective audit being conducted) the data and records contemplated under paragraph 9.3;  9.8.2  permitting the Customer, Customer Group, Auditors and/or Regulatory Authorities to conduct an audit or inspection on site at the Supplier's Site, provided that the Customer has notified the Supplier seven (7) days in advance (or such lesser time period if required to comply with Applicable Law or direction from a Regulatory Authority or as may be necessary if there is an emergency or crisis situation or where such period of notice would result in an ineffective audit being conducted); and  9.8.3  permitting the Customer, Customer Group, Auditors and/or Regulatory Authorities to make copies of relevant data and records (whether physical or electronic) contemplated under paragraph 9.3.  Supplier conducted/engaged audits  9.9  The Supplier shall make available to the Customer, at no additional charge, the results of any internal or external review or audit conducted by the Supplier or its Subcontractors, to the extent relevant to the Supplies provided by the Supplier under the Contract Document, within thirty (30) days after receipt or finalisation of the report in respect of such review or audit.   9.10  The Supplier shall undertake an annual internal audit of the Supplies and the internal control environment for them, with focus on:  9.10.1  compliance with the Supplier's policies and standards;  9.10.2  compliance with agreed controls for the provision of the Supplies;  9.10.3  compliance with Applicable Law; and   9.10.4  compliance with the requirements in the Contract Document.  9.11  The Supplier shall ensure that it and its Subcontractors have in place effective and consistent internal risk and contingency planning policies and procedures relating to the performance and observance of the Supplies.   Costs  9.12  Subject to paragraph 9.9, each Party will bear its own costs associated with audits and inspections.   9.13  If an audit or inspection reveals that the Supplier has breached the Contract Document (other than in a trivial or inconsequential manner), then the Supplier shall reimburse the Customer within thirty (30) days of receipt of a demand from the Customer, for the reasonable costs and expenses incurred by the Customer and/or Customer Group in connection with the audit and inspection.  Remediation  9.14  Where an audit or inspection reveals that the Supplier is not complying with any obligation under the Contract Document or risks are identified in respect of the manner in which the Supplier is performing its obligations, then if requested by the Customer, the Supplier shall take, and shall ensure that Subcontractors take, such actions as are necessary to promptly remedy the non compliance or to mitigate the risk.  9.15  This paragraph 9 shall survive the termination or expiry of the Contract Document.    Clause 5 (Audit)   Outsourcing Annexure    5.1  In addition to the requirements contained in the paragraph [7] (Audit) of the Regulatory Schedule, the Customer and its Auditors shall have the right to:  5.1.1  perform audits to verify the security and integrity of Customer Confidential Information and examine the Supplier Support Systems that Process, store, support and transmit Customer Confidential Information in connection with the Contract Document;  5.1.2  audit and inspect the Approved Locations, and any other premises from which the Deliverables and Services are being performed or provided, including any premises at which Customer Confidential Information is Processed;  5.1.3  run their own software on the Supplier Systems for the purposes of carrying out an audit or inspection.  5.2  Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, as part of an audit or inspection, the Customer or any Auditor may conduct:  5.2.1  penetration testing on the Services and any Supplier Systems; and  5.2.2  threat and vulnerability risk assessments in respect of the Services and Supplier Systems.  5.3  The Customer and the Auditor's rights of audit and inspection shall extend to the Supplier's Subcontractors.  5.4  The Supplier shall and shall ensure that the Subcontractors regularly conduct internal audits to ensure the adequacy of and compliance with the Supplier Group's risk and contingency policies and procedures.  5.5  The Supplier shall ensure that all Supplier Personnel are appropriately trained in the effective use of the risk management and contingency policies and procedures of the Supplier Group.      Clause 26.1.1 (Audit Management)   IT Outsourced Managed Services Schedule    26.  AUDIT MANAGEMENT  26.1  In addition to the Supplier's obligations set out in the Regulatory Schedule, the Supplier shall provide the following assistance to ensure the Customer is able to meet its regulatory and audit commitments:  26.1.1  respond within twenty four (24) hours (unless otherwise agreed in writing between the Parties) to audit requests for information and provide appropriate information to support the Customer's response.

TERMINATION  Termination for convenience  20.1  Subject to clause 20.2, the Customer may terminate the GTCs [or any Order Form] for convenience in its absolute discretion at any time by providing thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Supplier.   20.2  The Customer's sole liability in respect of termination under clause 20.1 will be the payment of:   20.2.1  the Charges for any Supplies provided to the Customer prior to the date of termination not included in any previous payment; and  20.2.2  any termination charges expressly identified and agreed in the Contract Document.

clauses.description: Termination for cause  20.3  The Customer may terminate the Contract Document with immediate effect, at any time by notice in writing to the Supplier, if:  20.3.1  the Supplier is in material breach of the Contract Document (being a single event or a series of events which are together a material breach) and either that breach is not capable of remedy or, if the breach is capable of remedy, the Supplier has failed to remedy that breach within thirty (30) days of receiving written notice from the Customer requiring it to do so;  20.3.2  the Supplier is in breach of the Contract Document (whether or not it is capable of remedy) which is persistent (and a breach is deemed to be 'persistent' if it is repeated sufficiently often to have a material impact on the use and enjoyment of the Supplies provided under the Contract Document or the businesses of the Customer, and similar breaches are deemed to be instances of the same breach);   20.3.3  the Supplier commits any breach of the Anti Bribery and Corruption provisions of the Regulatory Schedule, and any such breach shall be deemed to be incapable of remedy;  20.3.4  clause 7.3, 17.3 or 27.3 applies;  20.3.5  the Supplier or any Subcontractor commits a breach of security, confidentiality or data breach under clause 15, the Data Privacy Annexure and the Information Security Annexure to the Regulatory Schedule;   20.3.6  the Supplier, Supplier Personnel or the Supplier's professional advisers is in breach of the Charter and/or exhibit behaviour that, in the Customer's reasonable opinion, is misaligned to the Charter;  20.3.7  the Supplier receives a judicial indictment (in any country);  20.3.8  without the Customer's prior written consent, the Supplier is the subject of a takeover, merger, acquisition or other form of change in majority voting control (either at shareholder meetings or meetings of the board of directors);  20.3.9  an Insolvency Event occurs in respect of the Supplier;  20.3.10  the Customer's right to terminate the Contract Document is otherwise triggered under the Contract Document; or    20.3.11  there has been a demonstrable deterioration in the ability of the Supplier or a Subcontractor, to safeguard the Confidential Information.  20.4  If a Regulatory Authority of the Customer directs, instructs or gives guidance that the Customer should terminate all or part of the Contract Document or the continuation of the foregoing would cause the Customer or any other member of the Customer Group to be in breach of any Applicable Law, the Customer shall be entitled to terminate the Contract Document immediately without entitling the Supplier to receive any compensation in respect of the termination of the Contract Document. Upon such termination, the Supplier shall provide a pro rata refund to the Customer for Charges paid in advance for the period from the date of termination (if any).

clauses.clause_key: Exit Management & Data Return or Destruction
clauses.description: Clause 15.9   General Terms and Conditions    15.9  The Supplier shall upon the Customer's request, or as soon as such retention is no longer required for the performance of the Supplier's obligations under the Contract Document, delete, destroy or return the Customer Confidential Information and storage media in accordance with a process approved by the Customer. Upon the Customer's request, the Supplier shall certify in writing that deletion or destruction has taken place. Such deletion or destruction must be carried out in an appropriate and auditable manner that ensures that the Customer Confidential Information cannot be re created, accessed or read, including:  15.9.1  for electronic media that will be redeployed, a sanitation process that deletes data before redeployment, which destroys boot partitions, file pointers, and user data, and prevents all data from being reconstructed and read, in accordance with Good Industry Practice guidelines, for example, as set out in National Institute of Standards Technology (NIST) SP800 88 or US Department of Defence (DoD) 5220.22 M Directives; and  15.9.2  for disposal of documents, by cross shredding or using a secure waste disposal process designated for the Customer Confidential Information.      Clause 21.4.2 (Effect of Termination)   General Terms and Conditions    21.4  On the expiry or termination of the Contract Document, upon the Customer's request the Supplier shall as applicable:  21.4.2  immediately, or as otherwise advised in writing by the Customer, deliver to the Customer, or any party nominated in writing by the Customer, all property or Deliverables belonging to any Customer Group member (or any client or Customer Service Provider of a Customer Group member) that may be in the possession of, or under the control of, the Supplier or any of the Supplier Personnel, and, if any such property is in electronic form, the Supplier shall provide the Customer with unencrypted copies of the same in a format and on media reasonably requested by the Customer, and shall irretrievably destroy and delete copies so held in accordance with clause 15.9.     13.  EXIT MANAGEMENT   Regulatory Schedule   The Supplier shall comply with the requirements set out in the Exit Management Annexure attached to this Schedule.    Clause 6 (Access to Customer Confidential Information)   Outsourcing Annexure     6.1  The Supplier shall ensure that the Customer Confidential Information remains accessible by the Customer at all times during the Contract Document Term (including in the event that the Supplier files for bankruptcy, becomes insolvent, or makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors or in the event of discontinuation of the Supplier's business operations generally).    Clause 2.2 (Information Security)   Information Security Annexure    2.2  The Supplier shall upon the Customer's request, or as soon as such retention is no longer required for the performance of the Supplier's obligations under the Contract Document, delete, destroy or return the Customer Confidential Information and storage media in accordance with a process approved in writing or notified by the Customer. Upon the Customer's request, the Supplier shall certify that deletion or destruction has taken place. The Supplier shall perform such deletion or destruction in an appropriate and auditable manner that ensures that the Customer Confidential Information cannot be re created, accessed or read, including:  2.2.1  for electronic media that will be redeployed, a sanitation process that deletes data before redeployment, which destroys boot partitions, file pointers, and user data, and prevents all data from being reconstructed and read, in accordance with Good Industry Practice guidelines for example, as set out in National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) SP800 88 or US Department of Defence (DoD) 5220.22 M Directives; and  2.2.2  for disposal of documents, by cross shredding or using a secure waste disposal process designated for the Customer Confidential Information.    Refer to the attached Exit Management Annexure

clauses.description: Clause 47   (Step in rights)     IT outsourced Managed Services Schedule    47.  STEP IN RIGHTS  Rights to invoke step in  47.1  Without limiting the Customer's other rights under the Contract Document, if:  47.1.1  a Step In Event occurs;  47.1.2  the Supplier notifies the Customer that a Step In Event is likely to occur; or  47.1.3  in the reasonable opinion of the Customer a Step In Event is likely to occur,   then the Customer may give notice, in accordance with clause 47.2 below, requiring the Supplier to take such steps, as the Customer reasonably considers necessary or expedient in the circumstances, to mitigate or prevent the Step In Event (including the provision of additional or alternative Managed Services by the Supplier at the Supplier's cost and expense).   47.2  Notwithstanding any other provision of the Contract Document, and without prejudice to any other right or remedy of the Customer, if:   47.2.1  the Supplier fails to take steps within such reasonable time specified by the Customer or otherwise fails to remedy the Step In Event;  47.2.2  the Step In Event is an event that relates to:   47.2.2(A)  a Data Breach;   47.2.2(B)  a breach of confidentiality or security or the Information Security Controls;   47.2.2(C)  a breach of Applicable Laws;   47.2.2(D)  a direction or guidance from a Regulatory Authority; or  47.2.2(E)  [a Service Level Termination Event,]  47.2.3  the Step In Event may have a material adverse impact on the reputation of the Customer; or  47.2.4  the Customer reasonably considers that the Supplier is unable to remedy the Step In Event, or will not do so to the Customer's reasonable satisfaction,   then the Customer may, at its option, [and acting reasonably,] upon written notice to the Supplier ('Step In Notice'), do any of the following as the Customer considers necessary, and for as long as the Customer considers necessary, in respect of the affected Managed Services (the 'Impacted Managed Services'):   47.2.5  take control of or perform any of the Impacted Managed Services itself;  47.2.6  appoint a third party management team or engage a third party to perform the Impacted Managed Services; or  47.2.7  second employees ('Secondees') to the Supplier and such Secondees shall be given full access to all information that is available to Supplier Personnel in relation to the Impacted Managed Services. The Supplier shall act in accordance with Secondees' reasonable suggestions as to how to minimise disruption to the Managed Services,   (collectively, 'Step In').  Assumption of Control  47.3  If the Customer initiates a Step In then from the point in time of the Step In until the Supplier recommences performance of the Managed Services pursuant to clause 47.4:  47.3.1  the Customer shall not be required to pay any Charges for the Impacted Managed Services for that period;  47.3.2  the Customer shall, except where the Step In is in the form of Step In described in clause 47.2.7, be responsible for the performance of the Impacted Managed Services and the Service Levels which to apply to such Managed Services;  47.3.3  where such Step In has been taken following a breach or anticipatory breach of the Contract Document by the Supplier, the Supplier shall reimburse the Customer or the relevant Service Recipient for any excess costs (above those that would have been paid as Charges to the Supplier) incurred by the Customer or the relevant Service Recipient in conducting such Step In;  47.3.4  the Supplier shall co operate fully with the Customer or their nominee to facilitate the steps taken;  47.3.5  the Supplier shall take all reasonable steps to recommence full and proper performance of the Managed Services affected by the Step In Event;  47.3.6  the Supplier shall grant, and use reasonable endeavours to procure that any third party grants, the Customer or the relevant Service Recipient such licences as are reasonably required for itself for the purposes of exercising the rights under this clause 47;  47.3.7  the Supplier shall procure the reasonable co operation of and access to Supplier Personnel (including procuring that Supplier Personnel follow the reasonable instruction of the Customer Personnel or Third Parties appointed under this clause 47);  47.3.8  the Supplier shall procure that any Customer Personnel and Third Parties appointed under this clause 47 have access to:  47.3.8(A)  the books, records, information, the Supplier Support Systems and data under the control or possession of the Supplier and the Subcontractors which relate to the Managed Services and which are relevant to the activities covered by the Step In; and  47.3.8(B)  such of its Service Locations as are reasonably necessary in the circumstances.  Step out  47.4  Following the Supplier demonstrating to the Customer's satisfaction that the Supplier is ready to recommence performance of the Managed Services, the Supplier shall recommence performance in accordance with a timetable reasonably proposed by the Supplier, subject to the Customer's approval.  47.5  If the Supplier is unable to demonstrate to the Customer's satisfaction that the Supplier is ready to recommence performance of the Managed Services within [ninety (90) days] following the date of issuance of the Step In Notice, then without prejudice to the Customer's other rights and remedies under the Contract Document, Applicable Laws or otherwise, the Customer shall be entitled to terminate the Contract Document.        Clause 43 (Employees Transfer Regulations)    IT Outsourced Managed Services Schdule     43.  EMPLOYEE TRANSFER REGULATIONS  43.1  The Parties do not consider or intend that any Employee Transfer Regulations shall operate to transfer the employment, any personnel liabilities or any Employment Related Claims of any Supplier Personnel to the Customer or any Service Recipient or any Third Party supplier to the Customer or any Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors) in connection with the commencement, subsistence, termination or expiry of the Services.  43.2  The Supplier warrants that no Supplier Personnel or Employment Related Claims will transfer to the Customer or any Service Recipient or any Third Party supplier to the Customer or any Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors) in connection with or related to the commencement, subsistence, termination or expiry of the Contract Document or the provision of any Supplies under the Contract Document and that the Supplier is responsible for all the Supplier Personnel and any Employment Related Claims of or related to any such Supplier Personnel.  43.3  If it is claimed or is deemed that any rights or liabilities, including any Employment Related Claims arising from or related to any Supplier Personnel, transfer to the Customer or any Service Recipient or any Third Party supplier to the Customer or any Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors) at any time then the Customer or the relevant Service Recipient or Third Party supplier to the Customer or the Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors) may immediately terminate any such right or liability (including any contract of employment). The Supplier shall indemnify and fully reimburse the Customer or the relevant Service Recipient or any Third Party supplier to the Customer or any Replacement Supplier and their respective subcontractors from and against all Employment Related Claims incurred as a result of any such claim, transfer or termination otherwise incurred in respect of any Supplier Personnel.   43.4  The Supplier agrees that the Customer or the relevant Service Recipient may transfer the benefit of this clause to any Third Party supplier to the Customer or any Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors).  43.5  Notwithstanding the above, the Order Form will, as appropriate, include terms agreed between the Customer and the Supplier that will govern the rights and obligations of each Party in the event of a transfer of employment of Customer Personnel to the Supplier under the Order Form. The Supplier agrees to take reasonable steps to facilitate the application of any transfer of employment legislation to the transfer of Customer Personnel to the Supplier.

clauses.description: Clause 19 (Transfer of Undertakings)     19.  TRANSFER OF UNDERTAKINGS  19.1  Unless otherwise stated in the Order Form, the Parties do not consider or intend that any Employee Transfer Regulations shall operate to transfer the employment, any personnel liabilities or any Employment Related Claims of any Supplier Personnel to the Customer or any Customer Service Providers or any Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors) in connection with the commencement, subsistence, termination or expiry of the Services.  19.2  The Supplier warrants that no Supplier Personnel or Employment Related Claims will transfer to the Customer or any Customer Service Providers or any Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors) in connection with or related to the commencement, subsistence, termination or expiry of any Order Form or the provision of any Services under any Order Form and that the Supplier is responsible for all the Supplier Personnel and any Employment Related Claims of, or related to, any such Supplier Personnel.  19.3  If it is claimed or is deemed that any rights or liabilities, including any Employment Related Claims arising from or related to any Supplier Personnel, transfer to the Customer or any Customer Service Provider or any Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors) at any time, then the Customer or the Customer Service Provider or the Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors) may immediately terminate any such right or liability (including any contract of employment). The Supplier shall indemnify, keep indemnified and fully reimburse the Customer or any Customer Service Provider or any Replacement Supplier and their respective subcontractors from and against all Employment Related Claims incurred as a result of any such claim, transfer or termination otherwise incurred in respect of any Supplier Personnel. This clause 19.3 shall survive the termination or expiry of the Contract Document.  19.4  The Supplier agrees that the Customer may transfer the benefit of this clause 19 to any Customer Service Provider or any Replacement Supplier (or their respective Subcontractors).  19.5  Notwithstanding the above, the Order Form will, as appropriate, include terms agreed between the Customer and the Supplier that will govern the rights and obligations of each Party in the event of a transfer of employment of Customer Personnel to the Supplier under the Order Form. The Supplier agrees to take reasonable steps to facilitate the application of any transfer of employment legislation to the transfer of Customer Personnel to the Supplier.      Clause 21 (Step In Rights)     21.  STEP IN RIGHTS  Rights to invoke step in  21.1  Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies the Customer may have under the Contract Document or under Applicable Law, if:  (a)  a Step In Event occurs;  (b)  the Supplier notifies the Customer that a Step In Event is likely to occur; or  (c)  in the reasonable opinion of the Customer a Step In Event is likely to occur,  then the Customer may give notice to the Supplier, in accordance with clause 21.2 below, requiring the Supplier to take such steps as the Customer reasonably considers necessary or expedient in the circumstances, to mitigate or prevent the Step In Event at the Supplier's cost and expense, provided that if the Customer exercises its rights pursuant to clause 21.2, the Parties shall promptly commence good faith discussions regarding the Supplier's costs in respect of such impacted Services.  21.2  Notwithstanding any other provision of the Contract Document, and without prejudice to any other right or remedy of the Customer, if:  (a)  the Supplier fails to take steps within such reasonable time specified by the Customer or otherwise fails to remedy the Step In Event;  (b)  the Step In Event may have a material adverse impact on the reputation of the Customer; or  (c)  the Customer reasonably considers that the Supplier is unable to remedy the Step In Event, or will not do so to the Customer's reasonable satisfaction,  then the Customer may, at its option, do any of the following as the Customer considers necessary, and for as long as the Customer considers necessary, in respect of the affected Services (the 'Impacted Services'):  (d)  take control of or perform any of the Impacted Services itself;  (e)  appoint a Third Party management team or engage a Third Party to perform the Impacted Services; or  (f)  second employees ('Secondees') to the Supplier and such Secondees shall be given full access to all information that is available to Supplier Personnel in relation to the Impacted Services. The Supplier shall act in accordance with the Secondees' reasonable suggestions as to how to minimise disruption to the Impacted Services,   (collectively, 'Step In').  Assumption of Control  21.3  If the Customer initiates a Step In, then from the point in time of the Step In until the Supplier recommences performance of the Services pursuant to clause 21.4:  (a)  the Customer shall not be required to pay any Charges for the Impacted Services for that period;  (b)  the Customer shall be responsible for the performance of the Impacted Services and the Service Levels which apply to such Services;  (c)  the Customer shall ensure that where a Third Party is appointed in accordance with clause 21.2(e), such Third Party is subject to a reasonable written confidentiality undertaking or non disclosure agreement with the Customer to treat and keep Confidential Information confidential on terms no less onerous than contained in the Contract Document;  (d)  where such Step In has been taken following a breach or anticipatory breach of the Contract Document by the Supplier, the Supplier shall reimburse the Customer for any excess costs (above those that would have been paid as Charges to the Supplier) incurred by the Customer in conducting such Step In;  (e)  the Supplier shall co operate fully with the Customer or their nominee to facilitate the steps taken;  (f)  the Supplier shall take all reasonable steps to recommence full and proper performance of the Services affected by the Step In Event;  (g)  the Supplier shall grant, and use reasonable endeavours to procure that any Third Party grants, the Customer such licences as are reasonably required for itself for the purposes of exercising the rights under this clause 21;  (h)  the Supplier shall procure the reasonable co operation of and access to Supplier Personnel (including procuring that Supplier Personnel follow the reasonable instruction of the Customer employees or Third Parties appointed under this clause 21); and  (i)  the Supplier shall procure that any Customer employees and Third Parties appointed under this clause 21 have access to:  (i)  the books, records, information, the Supplier Support Systems and data under the control or possession of the Supplier and the Subcontractors which relate to the Services and which are relevant to the activities covered by the Step In; and  (ii)  such of its Service Locations as are reasonably necessary in the circumstances.  Step out  21.4  Following the Supplier demonstrating to the Customer's satisfaction that the Supplier is ready to recommence performance of the Services, the Supplier shall recommence performance in accordance with a timetable reasonably proposed by the Supplier, subject to the Customer's approval.  21.5  If the Supplier is unable to demonstrate to the Customer's satisfaction that the Supplier is ready to recommence performance of the Impacted Services within ninety (90) days following the date of issuance of the Step In notice, then without prejudice to the Customer's other rights and remedies under the Contract Document, Applicable Law or otherwise, the Customer shall be entitled to terminate the Contract Document for material breach.  21.6  Subject to the Supplier's compliance with its obligations in accordance with clause 21.2(f) (if applicable), the Supplier will not be liable for any degradation in the Impacted Services during the period when the Customer exercises the Step In.

clauses.clause_key: Incident Management  (Notification of Breach and Changes)
clauses.description: Information Security Annexure    Material Cyber Security Incidents   2.7  The Supplier acknowledges that the Customer is obligated to notify Regulatory Authorities of certain Material Cyber Security Incidents, and the Supplier shall promptly inform the Customer of a Material Cyber Security Incident without undue delay, and in any event no later than:   2.7.1  one (1) hour, if the Material Cyber Security Incident is a Relevant Incident; or  2.7.2  twenty four (24) hours for all other Material Cyber Security Incidents,  after the Supplier becomes aware of or reasonably suspects there has been a Material Cyber Security Incident.     2.7.2  two (2) hours for all other Material Cyber Security Incidents (for India Material Outsourcing),  after the Supplier becomes aware of or reasonably suspects there has been a Material Cyber Security Incident.  2.8  The Supplier shall inform the Customer of a Material Cyber Security Incident using the following communication channels:  2.8.1  Primary: the Customer's Cyber Defence Centre mailbox at security@sc.com; and  2.8.2  Secondary: the Customer's Cyber Defence Centre telephone number at +60 (3) 95489990,  (and such other communication channels notified in writing by the Customer to the Supplier from time to time), and shall provide the Customer with details of the Material Cyber Security Incident using the Customer's reporting template (as provided by the Customer to the Supplier from time to time), which shall include the following key information (the details in paragraphs 2.8.3, 2.8.4, 2.8.5, 2.8.6 and 2.8.7 are mandatory), and which the Supplier shall provide to the best of its knowledge at the time and as fully and comprehensively as possible:  2.8.3  the Supplier legal entity name;  2.8.4  the date and time of the Material Cyber Security Incident;   2.8.5  background and details of the Material Cyber Security Incident, including the volume and types of data affected, the likely consequences of the Material Cyber Security Incident, and the categories and approximate number of individuals concerned (if applicable);  2.8.6  a description of the measures taken or proposed to be taken by the Supplier to address the Material Cyber Security Incident (including where appropriate, measures to mitigate its possible adverse effects);  2.8.7  the relevant Customer contract manager contact for the Contract Document;  2.8.8  details of any Customer Confidential Information impacted as a result of the Material Cyber Security Incident, including the sensitivity of that Customer Confidential Information;   2.8.9  details of any network connectivity between the Customer and the Supplier;   2.8.10  details of the IP address of Supplier machines affected by the Material Cyber Security Incident; and  2.8.11  details of the domains in use at the Supplier's organisation.  2.9  In the event of a Material Cyber Security Incident, the Supplier shall assist the Customer in conducting any investigations and analysis that the Customer may require. The Supplier shall take all necessary steps consistent with Good Industry Practice, taking into account the severity of the risk, to resolve such Material Cyber Security Incident as quickly as possible and to prevent its recurrence. Without limitation to the foregoing, the Supplier shall:   2.9.1  perform a root cause and impact analysis for the Material Cyber Security Incident ('RCA');   2.9.2  subject to paragraph 2.9.3, promptly submit the results of the RCA to the Customer without undue delay, and in any event, not later than ten (10) days after the Supplier first informed the Customer of the Material Cyber Security Incident pursuant to paragraph 2.7;  2.9.3  if, due to ongoing analysis, complete reporting of results in the RCA is not possible at the time of RCA submission pursuant to paragraph 2.9.2:  (A)  the Supplier shall ensure the submitted RCA reflects as fully and comprehensively as possible the current state of the analysis at the time of submission; and   (B)  the Supplier shall request, and agree with the Customer, additional period(s) of time for the Supplier to submit further RCA revisions/updates/versions (reflecting as fully and comprehensively as possible the current state of analysis at the time of submission), provided that the final RCA with complete reporting of results is submitted without undue delay, and in any event, not later than twenty eight (28) days after the Supplier first informed the Customer of the Material Cyber Security Incident pursuant to paragraph 2.7; and  2.9.4  implement adequate corrective measures to prevent a recurrence of the Material Cyber Security Incident.  2.10  Without limiting paragraph 2.7, the Supplier acknowledges that the Customer may be obligated to notify Regulatory Authorities of Material Cyber Security Incidents. In such cases, notwithstanding any provision in the Contract Document to the contrary such notification shall not constitute a breach of the obligations of confidence under the Contract Document by the Customer.  Changes  2.11  The Supplier shall notify the Customer as soon as practicable from when it first becomes aware of:  2.11.1  any material changes to Supplier Systems retaining or hosting Customer Confidential Information on behalf of the Customer;  2.11.2  any change to the country location(s) or data centres where the Customer Confidential Information is retained, hosted or accessed from; or  2.11.3  any change in relation to Subcontractors engaged in Processing Customer Confidential Information in relation to the Contract Document.  2.12  The Supplier shall inform the Customer as soon as reasonably practicable (or such timescale agreed in writing between the Parties to meet the requirements of a Regulatory Authority) from when it first becomes aware of any relevant change of laws or regulations applicable to the Supplier or its Subcontractors affecting the Supplier's ability to comply with the terms of the Contract Document in relation to Processing Customer Confidential Information.
